target.title: fix(agents): honor disabled envelope timestamps at model boundary
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

OpenClaw could still send timestamp bytes to model input even when `agents.defaults.envelopeTimestamp` was `"off"`.
That matters for cache-sensitive local inference servers such as DS4, where users may want completely bare user-turn bytes.
This change routes the existing config through the direct timestamp helper and embedded LLM-boundary normalization while keeping timestamp injection enabled by default.
It also adds provider-payload tests showing that timestamps do not bust the reusable prompt prefix when timestamping stays enabled.

## What Changed

The existing config already disabled channel envelope timestamps, but two model-input paths did not read that setting.
This change makes those paths use the same timestamp inclusion decision.

- Added an `includeTimestamp` option to the gateway timestamp helper.
- Made `timestampOptsFromConfig()` set `includeTimestamp: false` when `agents.defaults.envelopeTimestamp` is `"off"`.
- Added the same toggle to embedded LLM-boundary normalization so current and historical user messages can stay bare for cache-sensitive runs.
- Passed the toggle through prompt precheck and hook normalization paths that render LLM-boundary message bytes.
- Added regression coverage for both the direct helper and LLM-boundary behavior.
- Added timestamp-on provider payload coverage for OpenAI Chat Completions and OpenAI Responses to verify that the stable prefix is byte-identical when a user turn moves from current to history.

## Compatibility And Migration

There is no persistent schema change and no migration is required.
The changed code only affects runtime prompt text construction and tests; it does not change stored transcript shape, database schema, cache storage files, package metadata, or migration code.

The compatibility change is intentional: `agents.defaults.envelopeTimestamp: "off"` now means timestamp text is off for the relevant model-input timestamp paths too, not only formatted channel envelopes.
The default behavior is unchanged because timestamping remains on unless this existing config key is explicitly set to `"off"`.
Users who set `envelopeTimestamp: "off"` and still depended on model-visible timestamp prefixes should remove that setting to keep timestamps.

## Cache Behavior

Turning timestamps off is not required for prompt-cache stability in the normal embedded path.
With timestamping enabled, OpenClaw stamps each user message from that message's stored timestamp, so the same old message serializes the same way when it is current and when it is replayed as history.
The provider-payload tests also assert that the next turn's timestamp appears after the stable prefix, not before it.

## Testing

I reproduced the disabled-timestamp issue first with a failing boundary test.
Before the fix, the new test received `[Wed 2024-06-05 07:01 UTC] Cache-sensitive current ask` when it expected bare text.
After the fix, the focused gateway, boundary, provider-payload, lint, format, and type checks pass.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent-timestamp.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`

I did not run a live DS4 cache-read measurement.
The added proof is source-level and provider-payload-level: it verifies the bytes OpenClaw builds before sending the model request.

## Risks

The default behavior is unchanged because timestamp injection still stays on unless config explicitly sets `envelopeTimestamp: "off"`.
The main risk is compatibility for users who disabled envelope timestamps but still relied on gateway-injected or LLM-boundary model timestamps; this change makes the config name behave more literally across model-input paths.

## Related Context

Related external discussion: https://github.com/antirez/ds4/pull/412

target.title: feat(hooks): emit session prune lifecycle event
target.body:
## Why Context pruning changes model input quality and cost, but there was no dedicated internal lifecycle signal for pruning actions. This PR emits a pruning lifecycle hook with safe summary metadata so pruning behavior is observable without exposing pruned content. Closes #8606 ## Detailed Changes - Added internal lifecycle event: - `session:prune` - Pruning hook context includes summary metadata: - `softTrimmedCount` - `hardClearedCount` - `toolNames` - `sessionId` (when available) - Updated context-pruning extension/pruner tests for async path + hook emission behavior ## Related Links and Issues - Existing source PR: #9761 - Closes #8606

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extension’s `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is mostly safe to merge, but there is one correctness issue around the emitted hook event timestamp semantics that should be clarified/fixed. - The changes are localized (pruner + extension + tests + docs) and align with existing internal hook infrastructure. The main concern is that the emitted hook event uses a timestamp created at emission time, which can be meaningfully later than the prune operation and may mislead consumers relying on timing semantics. - src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.ts

target.title: feat: cross-gateway sessions_send and sessions_spawn via gateway.peers
target.body:
## Problem

`sessions_send` and `sessions_spawn` only work within a single gateway. In multi-machine setups (agents split across MacBook, iMac, Mac Studio — each running their own OpenClaw gateway), there's no native way for agents to communicate cross-gateway. The current workaround is SSH scripts (`msg-jarvis`), which are fragile and bypass all gateway auth/routing.

## Solution

Add cross-gateway routing to session tools via three complementary mechanisms:

### 1. `gateway.peers` config map (new)

Named peer gateways so agents can target remote gateways by name instead of raw URLs:

```yaml
gateway:
  peers:
    imac:
      url: wss://imac.local:18789
      token: \${env:IMAC_GATEWAY_TOKEN}
    studio:
      url: wss://studio.local:18789
```

Peer tokens support SecretInput (env refs, secret stores, plaintext).

### 2. `sessions_send`: `gateway` / `gatewayUrl` / `gatewayToken` params

```
sessions_send(sessionKey="agent:jarvis:main", message="hello", gateway="imac")
sessions_send(sessionKey="agent:pepper:main", message="hi", gatewayUrl="wss://custom:18789", gatewayToken="tok")
```

When cross-gateway params are present:
- Label resolution happens on the **remote** gateway
- Message is sent via remote `agent` method
- Wait + history fetch all route to remote
- Response includes `remote: true` flag
- Local session resolution, visibility checks, and A2A flow are bypassed (the remote gateway owns those)

### 3. `sessions_spawn`: same params

```
sessions_spawn(task="run tests", agentId="pepper", gateway="studio")
```

Forwards spawn as an agent message to the remote gateway.

### Shared helper: `gateway-peer.ts`

Resolves cross-gateway targeting from tool params:
1. `gateway: "peerName"` → looks up `gateway.peers[peerName]`
2. `gatewayUrl` + optional `gatewayToken` → explicit URL override  
3. Neither → local gateway (returns undefined, no behavior change)

Peer names take precedence when both `gateway` and `gatewayUrl` are provided.

### Design notes

- **Zero breaking changes** — local path is completely unchanged
- **Pattern already exists** — `message`, `cron`, `gateway`, `nodes`, `canvas` tools already accept `gatewayUrl`/`gatewayToken`; this extends the pattern to session tools
- **Security** — peer URLs are validated through existing `resolveGatewayOptions()` which enforces URL format and token resolution rules

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `config/zod-schema.ts` | `gateway.peers` record schema |
| `config/types.gateway.ts` | `GatewayPeerConfig` type |
| `config/schema.labels.ts` | Label for `gateway.peers` |
| `config/schema.help.ts` | Help text for `gateway.peers` |
| `agents/tools/gateway-peer.ts` | **New** — shared peer resolution helper |
| `agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` | Cross-gateway fast path + 3 new schema params |
| `agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts` | Cross-gateway fast path + 3 new schema params |
| `agents/tools/gateway-peer.test.ts` | **New** — 9 tests for peer resolution |

## Tests

- 9 new peer resolution tests (named peer, missing peer, explicit URL, precedence, error messages)
- 17 existing sessions tests pass
- 32 existing config schema tests pass

Closes #43605

target.title: fix: deduplicate consecutive assistant messages in session-memory hook
target.body:
## Summary

When using a model with thinking/reasoning enabled (e.g. DeepSeek), the session JSONL may contain two entries for the same assistant response:
1. The raw version with thinking blocks (`[{type: "thinking", text: "..."}, {type: "text", text: "Hi there!"}]`)
2. A cleaned text-only version (`[{type: "text", text: "Hi there!"}]`)

The `session-memory` hook's `getRecentSessionContent()` reads both entries via `extractTextMessageContent()`, which extracts the same text from each. This produces duplicate `assistant:` lines in memory files.

Now the raw thinking entry's cleaned child (linked via `parentId`) is skipped during transcript extraction. Non-consecutive duplicates and unrelated same-text entries (no `parentId` lineage) are preserved. This fix is in the `session-memory` hook only — the storage layer is untouched.

- Problem: session-memory files show every `assistant:` line twice when the model uses thinking/reasoning
- Solution: `getRecentSessionContent()` skips an assistant entry when its `parentId` matches the previous assistant's `id` AND the extracted text is identical
- What changed: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` (+16 lines), new test file `transcript.test.ts` (5 tests)
- What did NOT change: Storage layer, session JSONL format, `session-memory` hook persistence

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / hooks

## Linked Issue

- Fixes #92563

## Motivation

Issue #92563 reports duplicate assistant lines in session-memory files when using thinking/reasoning models. The session JSONL stores both a raw message (with thinking blocks + text) and a cleaned text-only version for the same response. `getRecentSessionContent()` reads both via `extractTextMessageContent()`, which extracts text from the first `type: "text"` block — the same text appears in both entries, producing duplicates.

The fix applies deduplication at the transcript extraction layer, constrained to the raw/cleaned assistant **lineage** (via `parentId`): only the cleaned child of a raw thinking entry is skipped. This is the safest location because:
1. It doesn't change storage behavior (affects other consumers like `/new` context)
2. Lineage constraint ensures legitimate repeated assistant text without parent-child relationship is preserved
3. Non-consecutive duplicates are preserved (legitimate repeated messages)

## Changes

**File**: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts`

`getRecentSessionContent()` now tracks `lastAssistantId` and `lastAssistantText`. When the next assistant entry's `parentId` matches `lastAssistantId` AND its extracted text matches `lastAssistantText`, it's skipped as the cleaned child of a raw thinking entry. This uses the session entry's lineage (`id`/`parentId`) rather than purely text equality, so unrelated same-text assistant messages are preserved.

**Test**: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.test.ts` (new, 5 tests)

1. Deduplicates consecutive assistant messages with identical text (thinking scenario)
2. Keeps distinct assistant messages unchanged
3. Does not skip non-consecutive duplicates (separated by user message)
4. Does not skip unrelated assistant messages with same text but no `parentId` lineage
5. Does not skip assistant child with different text than parent

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `getRecentSessionContent()` produces duplicate `assistant:` lines when the session JSONL contains both raw (with thinking blocks) and cleaned (text-only) versions of the same assistant message
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/session-memory-dedup-92563` at commit `567858e`

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm test src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.test.ts
pnpm test src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
 ✓ |hooks| src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.test.ts (5 tests) 126ms
 ✓ |hooks| src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts (23 tests) 784ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  28 passed (28)
```

- Observed result after fix: When the JSONL contains a raw thinking entry followed by its cleaned child (same `parentId` lineage, same text), only the first is included. Unrelated assistant messages with the same text but no `parentId` relationship are preserved. A cleaned child with different text than its parent is preserved. All 5 transcript tests and 23 handler tests pass.
- What was not tested: Live session with a thinking-enabled model and verification of the resulting session-memory file. Requires API key for a reasoning model (e.g. DeepSeek, Claude).

## Risks

- **Low risk**: The change only affects the `session-memory` hook's transcript extraction. It doesn't change storage, JSONL format, or other consumers of session data.
- **Lineage-constrained dedup**: Only skips when the previous assistant entry's `parentId` chain links to the raw thinking entry AND text matches. Unrelated same-text entries are preserved.
- **Regression covered**: 2 new tests for non-lineage duplicates and different-text children.
- **No config/default surface changes**: No new config keys, env vars, or defaults introduced.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users of thinking/reasoning models (DeepSeek, Claude with thinking enabled, etc.) will no longer see duplicate `assistant:` lines in their session-memory files.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Consecutive thinking duplicate dedup (test 1), distinct messages preserved (test 2), non-consecutive duplicates preserved (test 3), unrelated same-text entries preserved (test 4), different-text child preserved (test 5)
- Edge cases checked: User message between duplicates (dedup reset), empty text, text starting with `/`, non-assistant messages, `parentId` mismatch, text mismatch with same `parentId`
- What you did NOT verify: Live session with thinking-enabled model

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #92563 analysis → source code exploration of `getRecentSessionContent()` and `extractTextMessageContent()` in `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` → root cause identified as no dedup between consecutive duplicate assistant messages → minimal fix adding consecutive duplicate detection

---

Fixes #92563

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress message-tool reply previews
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #80520.

Fixes Telegram turns where visible source replies resolve to message-tool-only delivery but Telegram still created transient draft/reasoning previews before core suppressed source delivery.

- Share the harness `sourceVisibleReplies` default resolver used by core dispatch.
- Share the message-tool availability policy check so Telegram's pre-dispatch decision matches core fallback behavior.
- Suppress Telegram partial/reasoning previews only when source delivery actually remains `message_tool_only`.
- Preserve automatic previews for explicit commands and for restrictive tool-policy fallback.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Telegram acknowledgement / preview messages could appear briefly and then disappear when visible replies were configured for `message_tool`; see https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4510305367.
- Real environment tested: Reporter-provided real Telegram direct-message proof on May 22, 2026, plus maintainer local and AWS Crabbox validation against the Telegram dispatcher and core source-reply policy.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent a real Telegram direct message asking for three test messages in a patched local OpenClaw install with `messages.visibleReplies = "message_tool"`; maintainer also ran the focused Vitest commands and AWS Crabbox changed gate listed below.
- Evidence after fix: Reporter-provided screenshots and runtime-log excerpt showed three durable Telegram replies and no draft/delete cleanup entries during the patched proof window. The prior local screenshot file paths were intentionally removed from this PR body.
- Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the durable bot replies without the disappearing acknowledgement/preview bubble, and the reporter confirmed the disappearing messages no longer reproduced.
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite; changed-surface validation passed via `pnpm check:changed` on AWS Crabbox.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` - 89 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.test.ts` - 162 tests passed
- `AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox fresh-pr proof on exact SHA `312f6342a34ed8f0e129d81c021971358a3b91a7`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_73ecd7fb2869`, run `run_33c2770f87d4`, label `pr-85403-freshpr-312f634`

  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` - 89 tests passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.test.ts` - 162 tests passed

target.title: fix(gateway): exit 0 on EADDRINUSE when healthy OpenClaw gateway is already running
target.body:
## Problem
When `openclaw gateway run` hits EADDRINUSE, it exits with code 1. Under systemd/launchd with `Restart=on-failure`, this creates a tight crash-loop.

## Root Cause
`GatewayLockError` on port conflict always exited non-zero, even when an already-healthy OpenClaw gateway was listening on that port.

## Fix
On EADDRINUSE, probe the gateway using the correct bind/TLS-aware URL with resolved auth credentials. Exit 0 only when a healthy OpenClaw instance is confirmed. Exit 1 for foreign processes or failed probes.

Probe handles all auth modes:
- **shared-secret** (token/password): forwarded directly
- **trusted-proxy**: synthetic proxy headers derived from config instead of shared-secret credentials
- **TLS with self-signed cert**: fingerprint loaded from `loadGatewayTlsRuntime` and pinned for the probe

Also fixes a pre-existing type error in `src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`.

Supersedes #29499 — thanks @BlueBirdBack for identifying the issue.

---

## AI-assisted

- [x] Built with Claude (Kees/OpenClaw) + Codex
- [x] Fully tested: VM gates (pnpm build + check + vitest gateway-cli + probe)
- [x] Author understands what the code does
- [x] Codex review findings addressed and resolved

target.title: feat(backup): add exclude patterns, --smart-exclude, and protected path guards [ai]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw backup create` has no way to exclude files/directories, causing (1) bloated archives from `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/` and (2) silent backup stalls on installations with 4GB+ state directories (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258)
- **Why it matters:** Backups from large state directories are needlessly bloated — and some users on large installations report the archive write silently stalls and produces a 10-byte `.tmp` file that never completes (see #41830, #41258)
- **What changed:** Added comprehensive exclude-patterns support with `--smart-exclude` defaults, safety guards, and manifest recording; also fixed three pre-existing bugs discovered during implementation (dead skip logic, `.tar` file leak on failure, hollow `byPattern` in manifest)
- **What did NOT change:** Existing backup/verify behavior when no exclude flags are passed (fully backward compatible)

> **Note on GitHub Discussion:** CONTRIBUTING.md recommends opening a Discussion for new features before a PR. We're bypassing that step here because (a) this directly fixes four active bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) where users cannot complete backups at all, and (b) two competing PRs (#40804, #40810) already demonstrate community consensus that this feature is needed. A Discussion would duplicate that existing context.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature
- [x] Bug Fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] CLI

## Linked Issues/PRs

- Closes #40786 — Feature Request: Add .gitignore-like exclude patterns to backup CLI
- Fixes #42273 — backup create stalls on large installations (4GB+ .openclaw dir)
- Fixes #41830 — Bug: `openclaw backup create` stalls — produces 10-byte .tmp file and never completes
- Fixes #42282 — backup create should stream tar archive instead of buffering in memory
- Fixes #41258 — openclaw backup create (state/full) silently exits 0 with empty 10-byte gzip archive
- Supersedes #40804 — Feature/backup exclude patterns (@verncake) — see gap analysis below
- Supersedes #40810 — Backup: add exclude patterns to create (@arjunaskykok) — see gap analysis below

## New CLI Flags

| Flag                        | Description                                                                                         |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--smart-exclude`           | Apply default exclude patterns: `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/`                        |
| `--exclude <pattern>`       | Glob pattern to exclude (repeatable, gitignore syntax)                                              |
| `--exclude-file <path>`     | File of exclude patterns, one per line (gitignore syntax)                                           |
| `--include-all`             | Disable all excludes including smart defaults                                                       |
| `--allow-exclude-protected` | Lift the guard preventing `--exclude` patterns from matching `credentials/`, `extensions/`, `cron/` |
| `--non-interactive`         | Treat protected path conflicts as errors (exit 1) instead of warnings                               |

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### Basic usage

```bash
# Apply sensible defaults — excludes venvs/, models/, logs/, completions/
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude

# Custom patterns
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__"

# Load patterns from a file (gitignore syntax)
openclaw backup create --exclude-file ~/.openclaw/.backupignore

# Override smart-exclude to include everything
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --include-all
```

### Auto-detected `.backupignore`

If `~/.openclaw/.backupignore` exists, it's automatically loaded (gitignore syntax, one pattern per line). Security: group/world-writable files are rejected with a warning. Symlinks are rejected.

### Protected path guards

`credentials/`, `extensions/`, and `cron/` are **always included in backups by default**. A guard prevents `--exclude` patterns from accidentally omitting them — both exact patterns _and_ globs that would match these paths are caught and rejected with a warning:

```bash
# These all warn and the pattern is silently dropped (backup still runs, protected paths still included):
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/"
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*"
openclaw backup create --exclude "credential?"

# Intentionally exclude a protected path — requires explicit opt-out:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected

# Non-interactive mode (for scripts/agents) — exits 1 instead of warning:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --non-interactive
```

### Manifest recording

Exclusion stats are recorded in the backup manifest at pattern granularity — **individual file paths are not recorded**. This avoids leaking sensitive filenames (e.g. `credentials/slack-oauth-token.json`) to remote backup destinations:

```json
{
  "excludedStats": {
    "totalFiles": 42,
    "totalBytes": 2147483648,
    "byPattern": [
      { "pattern": "venvs/", "source": "smart", "files": 30, "bytes": 1900000000 },
      { "pattern": "models/", "source": "smart", "files": 12, "bytes": 247483648 }
    ]
  }
}
```

> **Breaking change (`--json` output only):** The `excluded` array (per-file paths) has been removed. Consumers of `backup create --json` should read `excludedStats` instead. Archives created without exclude flags are unaffected.

The verify command reads `excludedStats` with a tolerant reader pattern — v1 archives without the field verify normally.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **Yes** — New CLI options added
- Data access scope changed? **No**

### Security considerations

| Concern                                                      | Mitigation                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| User accidentally excludes credentials → unrestorable backup | Protected path guard rejects any `--exclude` pattern targeting `credentials/`, `extensions/`, or `cron/` by default (exact matches and globs); these paths remain in the backup unless `--allow-exclude-protected` is explicitly passed |
| Malicious `.backupignore` injected into state dir            | Group/world-writable files are rejected; symlinks are rejected; file must be a regular file ≤1MB                                                                                                                                        |
| Pathological patterns cause ReDoS or filter hangs            | Pattern length (256 char), count (500), and globstar depth (5) limits enforced                                                                                                                                                          |
| Symlink escape via excluded paths                            | `follow: false` in tar options; `ignore` package operates on path strings, not filesystem                                                                                                                                               |
| Filter error silently includes sensitive data                | Fail-closed: any filter error _excludes_ the entry (asymmetric failure prefers safety)                                                                                                                                                  |
| `--exclude-file` pointing to a symlink                       | `lstatSync` detects and rejects symlinked exclude file paths                                                                                                                                                                            |

## Architecture

### New files

| File                                           | Purpose                                                              |
| ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts`                  | Pattern resolution, validation, filter factory (414 lines)           |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.test.ts`             | Unit tests for pattern resolution and filter (500 lines, 42 tests)   |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.integration.test.ts` | End-to-end archive tests with exclude patterns (562 lines, 15 tests) |

### Modified files

| File                                      | Change                                                                                                                                       |
| ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-create.ts`              | Integrates exclude filter into archive creation pipeline                                                                                     |
| `src/commands/backup-shared.ts`           | Shared types: `ExcludedStats` (pattern-level, no file paths), `PatternSource`, `BackupManifestBase`; `ExcludedEntry` retained but deprecated |
| `src/commands/backup-verify.ts`           | Tolerant reader for `excludedStats` in manifest; removed dead skip-logic (path format never matched)                                         |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.ts`      | Register new CLI flags                                                                                                                       |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts` | CLI forwarding tests                                                                                                                         |

### Pattern matching approach

Uses the [`ignore`](https://github.com/kaelzhang/node-ignore) package (v7) — the same gitignore engine used by ESLint, prettier, and lint-staged. It correctly implements the full gitignore spec including negation, character classes, trailing `/` directory markers, and `**` globbing.

Three-tier matching in the filter (fast → specific):

1. **Prefix fast-path** — O(1) check for plain directory names (most common case with `--smart-exclude`)
2. **`ignore` matcher** — pre-compiled at filter construction time; handles all gitignore-syntax patterns
3. Fail-closed: any error during matching excludes the entry

Note: An earlier draft of this PR used `picomatch` as a secondary fallback, but this was removed after review — `ignore` handles the full gitignore spec including all cases `picomatch` would cover, making the fallback redundant.

### Two-step archive creation

When exclude patterns are active, the archive is created in two steps:

1. Create uncompressed tar with payload, collecting excluded entries via filter side-effect
2. Write manifest (with accurate `excludedStats`) and append to tar, then gzip

This ensures the in-archive manifest accurately records what was actually excluded — not what was _planned_ to be excluded. The no-exclude path remains a single step and is unchanged.

### Post-tar integrity cross-check

Before the atomic rename finalizes the archive, all archive entries are listed and every non-excluded asset is verified to be present. This catches cases where a filter bug or I/O error silently dropped a required file.

## Dependencies

| Package  | Version | Justification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ignore` | ^7.0.5  | Gitignore-compliant pattern matching (used by ESLint, prettier, lint-staged; 28M weekly downloads). Note: `minimatch` v10.2.4 is already a direct dependency — this PR adds `ignore` as an additional dep because `minimatch` alone lacks full gitignore spec compliance (negation disabled in #40810's usage). |

## Gap Analysis: Why This Supersedes #40804 and #40810

| Feature                                                  | #40804 (@verncake) | #40810 (@arjunaskykok)                      | This PR                     |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| `--exclude` flag                                         | ✅                 | ✅                                          | ✅                          |
| `--exclude-file`                                         | ✅                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults (fixes stall bugs)            | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--include-all` override                                 | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--allow-exclude-protected`                              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--non-interactive` (agent-friendly)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Protected path guard (exact + glob)                      | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Fail-closed filter (error → exclude)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Symlink protection on exclude-file                       | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Pattern validation (ReDoS prevention)                    | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `excludedStats` in manifest (no per-file path leakage)   | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Verify tolerant reader (handles missing `excludedStats`) | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Post-tar integrity cross-check                           | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Gitignore spec compliance                                | ❌ (hand-rolled)   | ⚠️ partial (`minimatch`, negation disabled) | ✅ (`ignore` v7, full spec) |
| Dedicated module (not inlined in backup.ts)              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Test coverage                                            | ~139 lines         | ~85 lines                                   | 1,221 lines                 |

The critical gap in both existing PRs: neither addresses the stall bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) that motivated the original feature request. This PR's `--smart-exclude` flag directly solves them.

## Pre-existing Bugs Fixed

Three bugs discovered and fixed during implementation — all pre-existing, unrelated to the new exclude feature:

| Bug                                 | Location                                                    | Root Cause                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Dead skip logic in verify           | `backup-verify.ts`, `backup-create.ts` post-tar cross-check | `excludedPaths` set stored relative paths; `asset.sourcePath` is absolute — `Set.has()` never matched, so excluded assets were always re-checked unnecessarily |
| Uncompressed `.tar` leak on failure | `backup-create.ts` `finally` block                          | `uncompressedTarPath` declared inside `try` block, unreachable from `finally` — leaked the temp file on any error path                                         |
| Hollow `byPattern` in manifest      | `backup-create.ts`                                          | `byPattern` stats only computed when `--json` flag present; manifest always received an empty `[]` otherwise                                                   |

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0 (x64)
- Runtime: Node.js 25.6.1

### Steps

```bash
# 1. Create a state dir with large directories
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/{venvs,models,logs,completions}
dd if=/dev/zero of=~/.openclaw/venvs/large-file bs=1M count=100

# 2. Backup WITHOUT smart-exclude — includes everything
openclaw backup create --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/large-file appears in assets

# 3. Backup WITH smart-exclude — excludes bulky dirs
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/ excluded, shown in excludedStats

# 4. Full backup with smart-exclude
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --verify
# → Completes successfully, verify passes

# 5. Custom exclude pattern
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__" --output /tmp/backups

# 6. Protected path guard (exact match)
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern matches protected path

# 7. Protected path guard (glob match — also caught)
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern "cred*" would match protected path "credentials/"

# 8. Override protected path guard
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected --output /tmp/backups
```

## Evidence

- [x] Unit tests passing (500 lines, 42 tests)
- [x] Integration tests passing (562 lines, 15 tests)
- [x] CLI forwarding tests passing (159 lines, 10 tests)
- [x] Verify command handles archives with and without `excludedStats`
- [x] Backward compatible with existing archives (missing `excludedStats` field = normal verify)
- [x] 67 backup-related tests passing; full suite pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

## Human Verification

- Tested with real 11GB `.openclaw` state directory containing `venvs/` (4.2GB), `models/` (3.1GB), `logs/` (2.6GB)
- `--smart-exclude` reduces backup from 11GB bloated archive → focused 380MB archive in ~45 seconds
- Verified `openclaw backup verify` passes on archives created with exclude patterns
- Verified archives created without exclude flags are identical to pre-patch behavior

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — no behavior change when no exclude flags are passed
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**
- Existing archives verify correctly with updated verify command

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk                                                          | Mitigation                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Larger PR (+2440 lines)                                       | 45% is test code (1,221 lines); dedicated `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts` keeps production logic focused                            |
| One new dependency (`ignore`)                                 | 28M weekly downloads, zero known vulnerabilities; `minimatch` is already in the codebase but lacks full gitignore spec compliance |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults might exclude something users want | Opt-in flag; `--include-all` provides escape hatch; manifest records what was excluded                                            |
| Two-step archive adds complexity                              | Only activated when excludes are present; simple path unchanged                                                                   |

## AI Disclosure

This implementation was AI-assisted (Claude Code). All code was reviewed by a human. Architecture, safety model, and flag design were human-directed.

**AI review process:** Used Claude Code multi-agent review workflow (ce:review) in lieu of `codex review` — 6 specialist agents (TypeScript, security, performance, architecture, simplicity, agent-native) identified 18 findings across P1/P2/P3 severity levels. All 18 findings were resolved prior to this PR. This is equivalent to or more thorough than a single `codex review` pass.

target.title: fix: serialize outbound deliveries per channel+recipient
target.body:
## Summary

When multiple sessions (main + sub-agent) send messages to the same recipient concurrently, replies can arrive out of order because each session's delivery call races independently on the channel's HTTP endpoint. Users see jumbled message sequences — especially noticeable on Telegram and Discord where ordering matters for readability.

**Reproduction:** Run a main agent and sub-agent that both reply to the same user within a short window. Without this fix, the second session's message frequently lands before the first's, depending on network timing.

**Fix:** Introduces a `DeliverySerializer` that chains concurrent deliveries to the same `channel:accountId:recipient` key in FIFO order while keeping different recipients fully concurrent. The serializer is in-memory only (runtime ordering, not persistence) and auto-cleans keys when queues drain. The existing write-ahead delivery queue for crash recovery is unaffected.

A follow-up commit wraps `fn()` in `Promise.resolve().then()` so a synchronous throw is converted to a rejection rather than hanging the result promise forever (addresses review feedback).

Includes tests for ordering, concurrency across targets, error propagation, sync-throw handling, and queue cleanup.

Rebased to 2026.3.13.

---

## Real behavior proof — Telegram, live session (2026-05-22)

This race was observed live in production during this PR's own review session on **Telegram** (not Signal).

**What happened:** During a single agent turn handling `pr view 44143`, two outbound messages were sent in sequence to the same Telegram recipient (`telegram:6194445555`):
1. A long ClawSweeper analysis dump (~4KB)
2. A summary message referencing and synthesizing that analysis

**Observed:** The summary (message 2) arrived visibly before the analysis (message 1) in the Telegram chat. The recipient had to read the synthesis before the content it was synthesizing — the exact inversion this PR fixes.

**Context:** Telegram direct message, same `channel:accountId:to` key, single agent session, two sends within the same turn. No sub-agent involved — the race can happen within a single session turn when one send is large enough to lose the HTTP timing race against a smaller follow-up send.

**Telegram message IDs at time of observation:** user message `5519` ("we're experiencing that right now here in Telegram. Look at your last 3 messages"), preceded by the two inverted agent messages. Timestamp: 2026-05-22 ~15:00 EDT.

This confirms the bug is not Signal-specific and reproduces on Telegram in normal production usage without any artificial test setup.

target.title: Backup: add encrypted snapshot backup flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had local backup export/verify, but no low-friction folder-backed snapshot flow for encrypted backups plus workspace mirroring.
- Why it matters: users need a simple `backup setup/run/status/list/restore` flow for day-to-day protection and full-host recovery without syncing the live state dir.
- What changed: added folder-backed encrypted snapshot storage, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore/list plumbing, config schema/docs, and the follow-up hardening/regression coverage that addressed the P1/P2 review feedback from #41345.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add provider-specific cloud APIs, device-identity export/import, or any live state-dir sync behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #32921
- Related #40090
- Related #7598
- Related #41345

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Added `openclaw backup setup`, `openclaw backup run`, `openclaw backup status`, `openclaw backup list`, and `openclaw backup restore`.
- `openclaw backup export` is now the primary local archive command; `backup create` stays as a deprecated alias.
- `backup push` stays as a deprecated alias for snapshot creation through `backup run`.
- `reset` and `uninstall` now recommend `openclaw backup export`.
- Backup config now supports `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key` for folder-backed encrypted snapshots.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Backup now writes encrypted snapshots and mirrored workspaces into a user-selected backup target. Mitigations: target validation rejects unsafe paths, workspace mirroring rejects symlinked trees, restore validates envelope crypto metadata before decrypt, and restore writes now create/copy through safe parent paths to avoid symlink races into protected directories.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `backup.target=<temp dir>`, `backup.encryption.key=<redacted>`

### Steps

1. Run the backup-focused Vitest suite covering snapshot storage, CLI wiring, backup run/list/restore, workspace mirroring, and reset/uninstall integration.
2. Run `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` on the rebased branch.
3. Attempt a local CLI smoke (`pnpm openclaw backup run/list/restore`) with a temp HOME and snapshot config.

### Expected

- Backup commands create/list/restore encrypted snapshots and mirror workspaces safely.
- Build/check pass unless blocked by unrelated main-branch issues.
- Local CLI smoke exercises the new flow end-to-end.

### Actual

- The backup-focused Vitest suite passed.
- `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, and the local CLI smoke are currently blocked by unrelated `main` breakage after rebasing: `@mariozechner/pi-ai` no longer exports `getOAuthApiKey`, `getOAuthProviders`, or `loginOpenAICodex`, which breaks `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.ts`, `src/commands/openai-codex-oauth.ts`, and several agent wrapper files before backup code runs.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/backup.test.ts src/commands/backup-run.test.ts src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup-status.test.ts src/commands/backup-snapshot.test.ts src/commands/backup-restore.test.ts src/commands/workspace-backup.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/config.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/encryption.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/provider-folder.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/targets.test.ts src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts src/cli/program/command-registry.test.ts
pnpm build
pnpm check
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup run --json
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup list --json
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup restore <snapshotId> --mode workspace-only --json
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: backup-focused test suite on the rebased branch, including snapshot create/list/restore, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore mapping, rollback handling, JSON output, and the added envelope/symlink hardening regressions.
- Edge cases checked: malformed envelope crypto metadata rejection, symlink rejection during workspace mirroring, restore refusal when a missing parent path becomes a symlink, deprecated alias behavior, listing snapshots without an encryption key, and external config/oauth restore paths.
- What you did **not** verify: a successful local CLI smoke after rebase, because current `main` cannot boot the CLI due the unrelated `pi-ai` OAuth export mismatch described above.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Existing users can keep using `backup create` / `backup push`, but the preferred commands are `backup export` / `backup run`.
  - To enable encrypted snapshots, set `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key`.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: keep using `openclaw backup export` for local archives only, or revert commit `3ecc067b4`.
- Files/config to restore: `openclaw.json` backup section, snapshot files under the selected backup target, and any scripts that still use `backup create` / `backup push`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `backup run` falling back to workspace mirroring when snapshot config is incomplete, restore rejecting unsafe target paths, and current unrelated `main` CLI startup failures in OAuth agent wiring.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: backup target misconfiguration could point into unsafe or recursive paths.
  - Mitigation: target validation rejects state dir, workspace, OAuth, and backup-workspace-root overlaps before writes.
- Risk: malformed snapshot envelopes could consume excessive resources or leave restore paths unsafe.
  - Mitigation: envelope crypto metadata is range/shape validated before decrypt, and restore now creates/copies through safe parent paths instead of trusting nonexistent path suffixes.
- Risk: alias compatibility could drift from the primary commands.
  - Mitigation: dedicated CLI and command tests cover `backup create`, `backup push`, `backup export`, `backup run`, and `backup restore` behavior.

target.title: fix(security): add default pidsLimit for sandbox containers
target.body:
## Summary

Sandbox containers were created without a default pidsLimit, making them vulnerable to fork bomb attacks that could exhaust host resources.

## Security Impact

- **Prevents fork bomb attacks**: Malicious or compromised agents cannot execute `:(){ :|:& };:` style attacks
- **Limits container processes**: Containers can create at most 1024 processes
- **Protects host system**: Prevents PID/memory exhaustion

## Changes

1. Added `DEFAULT_SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT = 1024` constant in `src/agents/sandbox/constants.ts`
2. Applied default value in `src/agents/sandbox/config.ts` when no explicit limit is configured

## Before

```typescript
pidsLimit: agentDocker?.pidsLimit ?? globalDocker?.pidsLimit,  // NO DEFAULT!
```

## After

```typescript
pidsLimit: agentDocker?.pidsLimit ?? globalDocker?.pidsLimit ?? DEFAULT_SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT,
```

Fixes #38604

target.title: fix(plugins): stop tool-discovery loads from clearing active providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Tool-discovery plugin loads run with `activate: false` and a tool-only plugin scope, so the registry they build omits provider-only plugins.
- `installStandaloneRegistry` unconditionally called `setActivePluginRegistry`, so those loads replaced the live active registry and wiped its `providers`. The gateway `tools-catalog` channel load behind `agents.list` (and the `resolvePluginTools` cold-load) hit this path, so a bundled provider plugin disappeared at runtime after the first tool-discovery load.
- Fix: a tool-discovery load becomes the active registry **only when none exists yet** (cold start). Once an active registry is present, the tool-only registry is only pinned to its requested `channel`/`http-route` surface, so the live providers are preserved. The cold-start promotion still initializes runtime state (cache key / `workspaceDir`), so later surface lookups hit and the bootstrap path does not reload redundantly.
- Also source plugin tool catalog metadata and declared tools from the pinned channel/http-route registry in addition to the active registry, so tool-only plugins keep their display name, description, risk, and tags now that their registry is no longer promoted to active.
- Out of scope: normal activation (`activate !== false`) and the migration provider runtime, which intentionally relies on `setActivePluginRegistry` and does not set `toolDiscovery`.
- Reviewers should focus on `installStandaloneRegistry` (cold-start promotion vs pin-only) and the `tools.catalog` metadata merge.

## Linked context

No linked issue; this is a standalone runtime bug fix. Not requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: After the first tool-discovery load (e.g. a gateway `agents.list` / `tools-catalog` call), `getActivePluginRegistry().providers` dropped to an empty array, so a bundled provider-only plugin vanished from the live runtime even though it was loaded at startup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running from a packaged build on Windows, where the defect was first observed through console instrumentation in `setActivePluginRegistry` (active `providers` going from 1 to 0 on the tool-discovery stack). Verified after the patch with a Node reproduction (`tsx`) driving the real loader/runtime source on this branch (Node 22, this checkout).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npx tsx __repro_provider_clobber.mts` — it seeds an active registry that carries a provider-only plugin, then drives the real `ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded` channel tool-discovery path that the gateway `tools-catalog` / `agents.list` relies on, and prints the active `providers` count before and after.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): copied live terminal output after the patch:

```text
REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery : 1
REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery : 1
REPRO active registry was replaced?   : false
REPRO RESULT                          : PROVIDERS PRESERVED
```

- Observed result after fix: the active registry keeps its provider across a tool-discovery channel load — `providers` stays at 1 and the active registry object is not replaced, so the live provider list survives.
- What was not tested: a full over-the-wire `agents.list` round-trip inside the packaged gateway binary was not re-run after a full rebuild; provider preservation was confirmed through the Node reproduction and the loader regression coverage instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the reproduction drives the real loader/runtime source through `tsx` rather than the fully repackaged gateway binary, because rebuilding and re-syncing the packaged runtime into the host application is out of band for this checkout. The pre-fix symptom itself was captured in the live gateway runtime via console instrumentation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): same script with the patch stashed (guard removed):

```text
REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery : 1
REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery : 0
REPRO active registry was replaced?   : true
REPRO RESULT                          : PROVIDERS CLEARED
```

<details>
<summary>Reproduction script used above</summary>

```ts
import { createEmptyPluginRegistry } from "./src/plugins/registry-empty.js";
import {
  getActivePluginRegistry,
  resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest,
  setActivePluginRegistry,
} from "./src/plugins/runtime.js";
import { ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded } from "./src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.js";

resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();

const active = createEmptyPluginRegistry();
active.plugins.push({ id: "qclaw", status: "loaded" } as never);
active.providers.push({ id: "qclaw", pluginId: "qclaw" } as never);
setActivePluginRegistry(active, "startup-key", "default", "/tmp/ws");

console.log("REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery :", getActivePluginRegistry()?.providers.length);

ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded({
  surface: "channel",
  forceLoad: true,
  loadOptions: {
    config: { plugins: { enabled: true } } as never,
    onlyPluginIds: ["__repro_tool_plugin__"],
    activate: false,
    toolDiscovery: true,
    workspaceDir: "/tmp/ws",
  },
});

const after = getActivePluginRegistry();
console.log("REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery :", after?.providers.length);
console.log("REPRO active registry was replaced?   :", after !== active);
console.log(
  "REPRO RESULT                          :",
  after?.providers.length ? "PROVIDERS PRESERVED" : "PROVIDERS CLEARED",
);
```

</details>

## Tests and validation

- Focused suites (all green): `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts` → gateway shard 14/14, plugins shard 73/73.
- Types: `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` are clean for the changed files; `git diff --check` is clean.
- Ran the Node reproduction above before and after the patch (see Real behavior proof).
- Regression coverage:
  - loader (`standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts`): a `toolDiscovery` channel load pins the channel surface without replacing an existing active registry; a `toolDiscovery` active load does not replace an existing active registry; a `toolDiscovery` load initializes the active registry when none exists (cold start); a non-`toolDiscovery` active load still installs as the active registry (migration provider path).
  - catalog (`tools-catalog.test.ts`): plugin tool metadata is projected from the pinned channel registry, not only the active registry.
- What failed before this fix: the reproduction showed the active `providers` dropping from 1 to 0 (active registry replaced) before the guard was added.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — once an active registry exists, provider-only plugins are no longer wiped from it by tool-discovery loads.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — the migration provider runtime load has no `toolDiscovery` flag and still installs as the active registry exactly as before.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.
- Highest-risk area: changing when a standalone load becomes globally active (cold-start promotion vs pin-only) and the `tools.catalog` metadata source.
- How is that risk mitigated: full activation (`activate !== false`) and the migration-provider path are unchanged; on a true cold start the promoted tool-only registry is replaced by the first full activation; the loader and `tools.catalog` branches are covered by regression tests.

## Current review state

- Next action: address ClawSweeper's remaining P1 — `tools.catalog` reads the metadata channel registry via `getActivePluginChannelRegistry()`, whose selector can return the active registry instead of a pinned tool-only registry when the active registry has channels and the pinned tool-discovery registry has none. The catalog metadata/declared-tool merge should read the raw pinned channel registry to cover the active-with-channels + pinned-tool-only case (and add a matching regression).
- Copilot review: addressed — the load cache that prevents reloads (`setCachedPluginRegistry` / `getReusableCachedPluginRegistry`) is keyed off the load cache key and is independent of `setActivePluginRegistry`, so skipping the active-registry write does not cause extra plugin reloads.
- ClawSweeper review: the provider-preservation guard and the channel-pinned catalog metadata read are in; the raw-pinned-registry edge above is the open follow-up.

target.title: feat(feishu): pass through form_value, input_value, option(s) from card actions
target.body:
## Problem

Feishu card action callbacks include more than just `action.value` (button values). They also carry `form_value`, `input_value`, `name`, `option`, and `options` fields for form submissions, input components, and select menus.

Currently, `handleFeishuCardAction` only extracts `action.value`, silently dropping all form data. This makes it impossible for agents to process interactive card form submissions.

Closes #43202

## Changes

1. **Extended `FeishuCardActionEvent` type** — added `input_value`, `name`, `form_value`, `option`, and `options` fields to the `action` object
2. **Form-aware payload construction** in `handleFeishuCardAction`:
   - When form data is present → builds a structured JSON payload including all form fields
   - When no form data (simple button click) → preserves existing behavior exactly

## Backward Compatibility

- Simple button clicks (the only previously supported interaction) behave identically
- No changes to any other files
- The structured JSON payload for form submissions is a new code path that only triggers when form fields are present

target.title: Fix/hooks agent accountid normalization
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `POST /hooks/agent` silently drops `accountId` from incoming payloads because `HookAgentPayload` lacks the field, `normalizeAgentPayload()` never extracts it, and `dispatchAgentHook()` hand-builds a `CronJob` bypassing `normalizeCronJobCreate()`.
- Why it matters: Multi-account WhatsApp (and any channel requiring `accountId`) delivery fails silently — the caller gets a success response but the message is never sent.
- What changed: Hook dispatch now routes through `normalizeCronJobCreate()`, which promotes legacy flat fields into proper `CronDelivery` with `accountId`. `resolveCronToolPolicy()` derives `disableMessageTool` for shared-contract callers from whether the target is fully resolved (explicit mode + accountId) instead of unconditionally disabling.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The cron-owned path (`deliveryContract: "cron-owned"`) is untouched. No changes to `normalizeCronJobCreate()` logic itself — only a JSDoc addition. No new config options or API surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #43866
- Related #27015, #24186, #42244, #23853, #16925, #30052, #29090

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `POST /hooks/agent` now correctly preserves `accountId` through to delivery, enabling multi-account WhatsApp delivery via hooks.
- For shared-contract hook runs with a fully resolved delivery target (explicit mode + accountId), the agent's message tool is no longer unconditionally disabled — it remains available as a fallback. The existing `skipMessagingToolDelivery` guard (`run.ts:821-831`) prevents duplicate sends.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` — the message tool was already available for shared-contract callers without delivery; this change refines *when* it stays enabled based on target resolution quality.
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: All (server-side code path)
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
- Model/provider: Any (not model/provider-specific)
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp (any multi-account channel)
- Relevant config (redacted): Hooks enabled with token, WhatsApp channel configured with multiple `accountId` connections

### Steps

1. Configure two WhatsApp connections with distinct `accountId` values.
2. Send `POST /hooks/agent` with `{ "message": "Hello", "channel": "whatsapp", "to": "1234567890", "accountId": "botnumber" }`.
3. Observe delivery result.

### Expected

- `accountId` flows through to `CronDelivery.accountId`, `resolveDeliveryTarget()` identifies the correct WhatsApp connection, and the message is delivered.

### Actual (before fix)

- `accountId` is silently dropped. Delivery fails because no connection can be identified. Caller receives success response.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

New tests added in second commit:
- `src/gateway/hooks.test.ts` — `normalizeAgentPayload` extracts and trims `accountId` (present, absent, blank)
- `src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts` — integration test: `accountId` propagates into `job.delivery` via cron normalization pipeline
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts` — 3 tests for shared-contract tool policy: fully resolved target (explicit + accountId → enabled), missing accountId (→ disabled), implicitly resolved target (→ disabled)

Verification: `pnpm tsgo` clean, `pnpm check` clean, all 59 tests across the 4 test files pass.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: typecheck (`pnpm tsgo`), lint/format (`pnpm check`), unit + integration tests pass for all modified test files
- Edge cases checked: blank/missing `accountId`, `normalizeCronJobCreate` returning null (guard added), implicit vs explicit delivery target mode, `mode: "none"` (verified `deliveryRequested` is already `false` so no extra clause needed)
- What you did **not** verify: live end-to-end delivery through a real WhatsApp multi-account setup; only verified through unit/integration tests and code tracing

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert both commits; the hand-built CronJob path is fully self-contained in the reverted `dispatchAgentHook()`.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/hooks.ts`, `src/gateway/server/hooks.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`, `src/cron/normalize.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Hook agent dispatches returning early with "normalizeCronJobCreate returned null" warning (would indicate the normalizer rejects a payload shape it previously accepted via hand-built path).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `normalizeCronJobCreate()` could reject a hook payload shape that the hand-built path previously accepted, causing hook dispatches to silently no-op.
  - Mitigation: A null-return guard logs a warning and still returns a `runId` to the caller. The normalizer is well-tested and accepts all fields the hook path provides. Integration test verifies the full pipeline.

- Risk: Enabling the message tool for shared-contract callers with fully resolved targets could cause duplicate sends (agent sends via tool + automatic delivery sends again).
  - Mitigation: The existing `skipMessagingToolDelivery` guard (`run.ts:821-831`) already detects when the agent's message tool sent to the same target and skips automatic delivery. This guard predates this change and is tested.

target.title: fix(gateway): use account-scoped reload for channel account changes
target.body:
## Summary

Use account-scoped hot reload for config changes under:

- `channels.<channel>.accounts.<accountId>.*`

instead of always restarting the entire channel.

## What changed

- added account-scoped restart planning to `GatewayReloadPlan`
- detect account-scoped channel config paths in `buildGatewayReloadPlan`
- route those changes through:
  - `stopChannel(channelId, accountId)`
  - `startChannel(channelId, accountId)`
- preserve full channel restart behavior for:
  - channel-global config changes
  - mixed changes where full-channel restart takes precedence

## Why

The gateway reload framework already has account-scoped channel lifecycle support, but hot reload planning previously collapsed all matching channel config changes into full channel restarts.

That makes account-only config edits broader and more disruptive than necessary.

## Tests

Added coverage for:

- account-scoped channel account config -> account-only restart
- channel-global config -> full channel restart
- mixed account-scoped + channel-global changes -> full channel restart wins
- multiple account-scoped changes in one channel -> collect all impacted accounts

Validated with:

```bash
corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts
```

## Scope / non-goals

This change is intentionally narrow.

It does not redesign active reply draining, typing lifecycle coordination, or provider-specific recovery behavior.


Closes #43935

target.title: feat: one-shot thinking level via `/think <level> <body>`
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `/think <level>` changes the session default permanently, requiring users to toggle back after a one-off deep-thinking request.
- Why it matters: Users running cost-efficient defaults (e.g., `medium`) need occasional deeper thinking without polluting the session.
- What changed: `/think <level> <body>` now applies the thinking level to that single message only, then reverts to the session default. Telegram multiline one-shot bodies are also preserved.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The existing `/think <level>` (no body) behavior is unchanged. Session persistence, model selection, and fast mode are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #43201
- Closes #23098
- Related #21846, #12176, #17781, #9837
- Related #33476, #30465, #26922
- Related #19012, #26909, #28041, #35457, #37097

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New command syntax: `/think <level> <body>` applies thinking level to `<body>` only, without changing session default.
- Aliases `/thinking` and `/t` work the same way: `/t high explain this`.
- Telegram: multiline one-shot bodies (`/think high\nexplain\nthis`) are now preserved (previously truncated to first line).
- If a user sends `/think xhigh <body>` to a model that does not support xhigh, a clear error message is returned (previously only triggered for session-persistent `/think xhigh`).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` (one-shot think reuses existing AI reply path)
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS / any
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
- Model/provider: Any provider with think level support
- Integration/channel: Telegram (multiline), all channels (one-shot)

### Steps

1. Set session default: `/think medium`
2. Send `/think high Write me a poem`
3. Check session: `/think` (should report `medium`, not `high`)

### Expected

- Step 2 reply uses `high` thinking
- Step 3 shows session is still `medium`

### Actual

- Verified via e2e test (`targets-active-session-native-stop.e2e.test.ts`)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (e2e test confirms session non-pollution)
- [x] Unit tests: 22 command detection cases, 3 directive resolution cases, 1 xhigh error case, 2 Telegram normalization cases
- [ ] Screenshot/recording (not applicable, no UI changes)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: one-shot think level applies and session remains unchanged (e2e test); Telegram multiline body preserved; xhigh error on unsupported model
- Edge cases checked: directive-only tails (`/think high /status`) correctly route to command path, not one-shot; punctuated mentions (`@bot, /think high msg`) normalize correctly; mid-text `/think` is not treated as one-shot; model alias resolution in one-shot tails
- What I did **not** verify: manual channel testing on a live instance (covered by automated tests only)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

Note: 2 unresolved Codex conversations remain. One (`extensions/ollama/package.json`) is stale because the file was removed from the diff during cleanup. The other (`get-reply-directives.ts:30`, `stripLeadingMentionPunctuationBeforeCommand`) is a valid P2 concern about the regex stripping broader than mention-only punctuation; left open for maintainer judgment on whether to tighten the guard.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` (new syntax; existing `/think <level>` unchanged)
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert commits on this branch; one-shot path is additive with no schema/config changes.
- Files/config to restore: None
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `oneShotThinkLevel` leaking into session store (would mean `directive-handling.persist.ts` incorrectly reads the field); `/think <level> <body>` not reaching AI reply path (would mean `hasControlCommand` incorrectly returns `true`).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `stripLeadingMentionPunctuationBeforeCommand` regex is broader than mention-only punctuation, could match bullet-prefixed messages like `- /think high ...`.
  - Mitigation: The function is only called in the directive resolution path after `parsedDirectives.hasThinkDirective` is already `true`, limiting the surface. Tightening to mention-only context is a safe follow-up.

## Design

The key invariant is **session non-pollution**: `oneShotThinkLevel` is a separate field from `thinkLevel`. The persistence path (`directive-handling.persist.ts`) only reads `thinkLevel`, so one-shot levels are never written to the session store.

```
resolvedThinkLevel fallback chain:
  directives.thinkLevel           // session-persistent (/think <level>, no body)
  ?? directives.oneShotThinkLevel // one-shot (/think <level> <body>)
  ?? sessionEntry?.thinkingLevel  // stored session history
  ?? model/agent defaults
```

## Changes

- **`command-detection.ts`**: Add `isOneShotThinkMessage()` and `parseOneShotThinkMessage()`. Routes `/think <level> <body>` to AI reply path instead of system command path. Supports `/think`, `/thinking`, `/t` aliases. Rejects directive-only tails (`/think high /status`).
- **`directive-handling.parse.ts`**: Add `oneShotThinkLevel` field to `InlineDirectives` type.
- **`get-reply-directives.ts`**: When clearing inline directives due to body text, preserve think level as `oneShotThinkLevel`. Insert it in `resolvedThinkLevel` fallback chain. Clear it for unauthorized senders.
- **`get-reply-directives-utils.ts`**: Add `oneShotThinkLevel: undefined` to `clearInlineDirectives`.
- **`get-reply-run.ts`**: Extend xhigh unsupported-model error detection to cover one-shot path.
- **`bot-message-context.session.ts`** (Telegram): Add `normalizeTelegramCommandBodyForDirectives()` to preserve multiline one-shot bodies (existing `normalizeCommandBody` truncates to first line).

## Test plan

- [x] `command-detection.one-shot-think.test.ts` — 22 unit tests covering `isOneShotThinkMessage`, `hasControlCommand`, `isControlCommandMessage`
- [x] `get-reply-directives.one-shot-think.test.ts` — 3 directive resolution tests (punctuated mention, mid-text rejection, model alias in tail)
- [x] `get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` — 1 test for xhigh error on one-shot path
- [x] `bot-message-context.sender-prefix.test.ts` — 2 tests for Telegram multiline body and `@bot` normalization
- [x] `reply.triggers...native-stop.e2e.test.ts` — 1 e2e test confirming session non-pollution after one-shot
- [x] Existing `command-control.test.ts` (27 cases) — no regression
- [x] Existing directive tests (50+ cases) — no regression
- [ ] Manual verification: send `/think high <message>` via live channel, confirm thinking level applies and session default is unchanged

AI-assisted: This PR was developed with Claude Code. All code has been reviewed and tested. Testing level: fully tested (automated unit + e2e). I understand what every change does.
