target.title: fix: copilot token cache ignores profile rotation on rate limit
target.body:
Closes #43658

## Problem

When multiple `github-copilot` auth profiles are configured, profile rotation after a rate limit has no effect. All profiles end up using the same Copilot API token because `resolveCopilotApiToken()` caches to a single shared file (`github-copilot.token.json`) without tracking which GitHub account produced the token. A profile switch passes a different GitHub PAT, but the cache returns the still-valid token from the rate-limited account.

## Solution

Store a SHA-256 fingerprint (first 16 hex chars) of the GitHub PAT alongside the cached Copilot API token. On cache lookup, compare the fingerprint against the current profile's token. A mismatch forces a fresh token exchange with the GitHub Copilot token endpoint.

### Changes

**`src/providers/github-copilot-token.ts`**
- Added `githubTokenFingerprint` field to `CachedCopilotToken` type
- Added `fingerprintGithubToken()` helper (SHA-256, truncated to 16 hex chars)
- Cache hit now requires fingerprint match in addition to expiry check
- New tokens are saved with the fingerprint of the GitHub PAT used

**`src/providers/github-copilot-token.test.ts`**
- Updated existing cache-hit test to include fingerprint
- Added test: cache bypassed when a different GitHub token (different profile) is used
- Added test: legacy cache entries without fingerprint are treated as cache miss

### Backward compatibility

- Legacy cache files without `githubTokenFingerprint` are treated as a cache miss — the token is re-fetched on the next call and saved with a fingerprint. No migration needed.
- The `githubTokenFingerprint` field is optional (`?`) so older versions reading a new cache file won't break.

## Testing

```
✓ derives baseUrl from token
✓ uses cache when token is still valid
✓ fetches and stores token when cache is missing
✓ bypasses cache when a different github token (profile) is used
✓ bypasses cache when cached entry has no fingerprint (legacy)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
```

All 38 auth-profiles tests also pass.

target.title: feat: configure metadata (contextWindow, maxTokens, etc.) for custom provider setup
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets: - Problem: `openclaw configure --section model` sets up providers and auth but never prompts for model metadata (contextWindow, maxTokens, reasoning, input types, cost). Users must hand-edit config or run many `config set` calls to configure these. - Why it matters: Custom/self-hosted endpoints default to contextWindow=16000 and maxTokens=4096, which are often wrong. Users have no interactive way to correct this during setup. - What changed: After custom provider setup in `promptAuthConfig`, the wizard now asks "Configure metadata for `/`?" and walks through the core `ModelDefinitionConfig` fields (identity, capabilities, limits, cost) with current values prefilled. Built-in providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) are unaffected. Their catalog already provides defaults. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): The existing provider/auth flow is untouched. The metadata prompt is purely additive (appended after the custom provider path only). No config schema changes. No new CLI commands. Declining the prompt preserves the previous behavior exactly. Built-in provider flows are not modified. Compat flags (supportsStore, supportsTools, etc.) are not prompted for; they use sensible runtime defaults when absent and can be set manually in config by advanced users. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `openclaw configure --section model` with a **custom provider**: after provider setup completes, a new confirm prompt appears: "Configure metadata for `/`? (context window, max tokens, etc.)". Answering Yes walks through all model metadata fields. Answering No preserves previous behavior. - **Built-in providers** (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, etc.): no change. The metadata prompt does not appear. - Fields prompted (custom only): display name, API adapter, reasoning, input modalities, context window, max output tokens, token pricing (gated behind its own confirm). - All fields prefilled with current values; pressing Enter keeps defaults. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin 25.2.0) - Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm - Model/provider: Custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint ([thegrid.ai](https://thegrid.ai/)) - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): Standard `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with custom provider ### Steps 1. Run `pnpm openclaw configure --section model` 2. Select "Custom Provider" and complete auth/model setup 3. Observe the new prompt: "Configure metadata for `/`? (context window, max tokens, etc.)" 4. Answer Yes and walk through the fields, or answer No to skip 5. (Optional) Repeat with a built-in provider (e.g. OpenAI) and confirm the metadata prompt does **not** appear ### Expected - After custom provider setup, a metadata configuration prompt appears - Answering Yes allows editing all core `ModelDefinitionConfig` fields - Answering No skips metadata editing (same behavior as before this change) - Updated metadata is written to `models.providers..models[]` in config - Built-in providers skip the metadata prompt entirely ### Actual - Works as expected ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) 18 unit tests in `src/commands/model-metadata.test.ts` covering: `collectConfiguredModels`, `findModelInConfig`, `findPrimaryModelEntry`, `applyModelDefinitionUpdate`, `formatTokenCount`, `promptModelMetadata`, `promptModelMetadataForPrimary` (including skip for built-in providers). All passing. Existing `configure.wizard.test.ts` tests also pass. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Ran `pnpm openclaw configure --section model` end-to-end with a custom provider (thegrid-ai), confirmed metadata prompt appears after model setup. Ran with OpenAI, confirmed metadata prompt does **not** appear. - Edge cases checked: Declining metadata prompt preserves config unchanged; missing primary model gracefully skips; provider with no models returns empty list; built-in providers silently skip the prompt - What you did **not** verify: All built-in provider auth paths (only tested OpenAI). Other built-in providers use the same `authChoice !== "custom-api-key"` code path so are expected to behave identically. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes`: declining the new prompt produces identical config to before - Config/env changes? `No`: writes to existing `models.providers.*.models[]` fields - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the three-line addition in `configure.gateway-auth.ts` (lines 138-140: the `if (authChoice === "custom-api-key")` block calling `promptModelMetadataForPrimary`) - Files/config to restore: `src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.ts` (remove import + call), delete `src/commands/model-metadata.ts` and `src/commands/model-metadata.test.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: If the metadata prompt throws, it would interrupt the configure wizard after custom provider setup but before config write. The `WizardCancelledError` path is handled by the parent wizard. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Metadata prompt adds extra steps to the custom provider configure flow, potentially annoying for users who don't need it. - Mitigation: Gated behind a confirm ("Configure metadata?") defaulting to Yes but skippable with a single keypress. Cost is further gated behind its own confirm.

target.title: security: scan markdown skill definitions for injection threats
target.body:
## Summary

The skill scanner (`src/security/skill-scanner.ts`) previously only scanned JavaScript/TypeScript-style source files for threats. This PR extends it to also scan Markdown `.md` files, including `SKILL.md` definitions, for injection and obfuscation patterns.

Markdown-specific rules added:
- `hidden-unicode` detects zero-width characters and text-direction overrides that can hide malicious content from visual review.
- `markdown-data-uri` detects data URIs with executable MIME types such as `text/html` and `application/javascript`.
- `markdown-download-exec` detects `curl`/`wget` download-and-execute pipelines, including common bypass forms like `sudo bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, line continuations, and quoted pipe characters inside arguments.
- `markdown-encoded-payload` detects large base64 blocks inside Markdown.
- `markdown-hex-payload` detects hex-encoded payloads inside Markdown.

Implementation notes:
- Added `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` and `isMarkdown()`.
- Added `.md` to `SCANNABLE_EXTENSIONS`.
- Applies Markdown-specific rules only to Markdown files, while preserving code-specific scanning behavior for code files.
- Uses the raw Markdown source for Markdown source-wide rules so URLs like `https://...` are not accidentally truncated by code-comment stripping.
- Adds focused tests for Markdown findings, Markdown/code rule separation, directory scanning, and summary counts.

Why this matters: skill definitions can include rendered Markdown. Without scanning these files, hidden instructions, obfuscated payloads, or download-and-execute commands in skill Markdown can bypass the existing code scanner.

Supersedes #10705 (auto-closed by stale bot). Rebased cleanly onto current `main`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct scanner smoke for Markdown download-and-execute bypass forms plus benign Markdown prose and table negative cases

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Markdown skill definitions such as `SKILL.md` are now scanned for hidden Unicode, executable data URIs, download-and-execute pipelines, and encoded payloads that previously fell outside the JavaScript/TypeScript scanner surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at `/Users/alexalaniz/Code/openclaw-43469`, based on current `openclaw/openclaw:main` (`24bd0b212f`), with dependencies installed using `pnpm@10.33.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the actual scanner through `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs` against Markdown sources containing `curl`/`wget` bypass forms, then ran `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, and `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
markdown scanner smoke passed 7 positives and 2 negatives

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  32 passed (32)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.08s

All matched files use the correct format.

Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

Observed result after fix: The actual parser-based scanner caught 7 Markdown download-and-execute bypass samples in the smoke run (`sudo bash`, `sudo -E bash`, `env FOO=1 bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, a backslash-newline before `| bash`, and a quoted pipe inside a curl header) and did not flag benign curl prose or `| curl | bash |` Markdown table cells, while the targeted scanner test file and changed-file gate completed successfully.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

target.title: fix(agents): suggest recovery for unknown tool ids
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Fixes #92273, where Tool Search `mode: "tools"` could leave a model stuck after guessing an id such as `file_write`; the unknown-id error was not actionable enough for the caller to recover before memory flush/compaction work completed.
- Solution: Keep unknown-id recovery guidance tied to the caller contract: raw Tool Search controls mention `tool_search` / `tool_describe` / `tool_call`, while Code Mode bridge callers see `openclaw.tools.search` / `openclaw.tools.describe` / `openclaw.tools.call`.
- What changed: Added a caller-specific recovery surface to the Tool Search lookup/formatter path, preserved short no-suggestion direct misses, added a Code Mode bridge regression for `file_write` -> `write`, and normalized raw QuickJS runtime interrupts to the existing Code Mode timeout contract.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not add a new Tool Search public API, config option, schema field, migration, compatibility alias, guessed-id execution path, MCP namespace exposure, or broad tool-matching behavior. Existing raw Tool Search control names and Code Mode bridge method names remain unchanged.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** A recoverable unknown-tool error lets the model search, describe, and retry with the exact available tool before durable session memory is lost; Code Mode users no longer receive instructions for raw structured tool names that are unavailable inside the bridge.
- Fixes #92273

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A model or Code Mode script that guesses `file_write` when the actual catalog entry is `write` now receives a recoverable error with the correct API surface for the caller. Raw Tool Search mode still receives raw control-tool guidance, and Code Mode bridge receives `openclaw.tools.*` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw workspace at commit `1a2d65eddd9d80e6eeed1e90778edd782e70131f` on Linux. The proof command imports and executes the production `applyToolSearchCatalog` / `createToolSearchTools` implementation with the real Code Mode child process bridge path.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { applyToolSearchCatalog, createToolSearchTools } from "./src/agents/tool-search.ts"; const resultText = (error) => error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error); const writeTool = { name: "write", label: "Write", description: "Write a file to the workspace", parameters: { type: "object", properties: { path: { type: "string" }, content: { type: "string" } } }, execute: async () => ({ content: [{ type: "text", text: "unexpected write" }], details: { called: true } }) }; const codeSession = { sessionId: "proof-code-guessed-file-write", sessionKey: "agent:main:main" }; const codeToolsForCatalog = createToolSearchTools({ ...codeSession, config: {} }); applyToolSearchCatalog({ tools: [codeToolsForCatalog[0], writeTool], config: { tools: { toolSearch: true } }, ...codeSession }); const [codeTool] = createToolSearchTools({ ...codeSession, config: {} }); try { await codeTool.execute("proof-code-mode", { code: "return await openclaw.tools.call(\"file_write\", { path: \"memory/2026-05-22.md\" });" }); } catch (error) { console.log("CODE_MODE_BRIDGE_ERROR=" + resultText(error)); } const rawSession = { sessionId: "proof-raw-guessed-file-write", sessionKey: "agent:main:main" }; const rawControlsForCatalog = createToolSearchTools({ ...rawSession, config: { tools: { toolSearch: { mode: "tools" } } } }); applyToolSearchCatalog({ tools: [rawControlsForCatalog[1], rawControlsForCatalog[2], rawControlsForCatalog[3], writeTool], config: { tools: { toolSearch: { mode: "tools" } } }, ...rawSession }); const rawTools = createToolSearchTools({ ...rawSession, config: { tools: { toolSearch: { mode: "tools" } } } }); try { await rawTools[3].execute("proof-raw-tools", { id: "file_write", args: { path: "memory/2026-05-22.md", content: "remember this" } }); } catch (error) { console.log("RAW_TOOL_CALL_ERROR=" + resultText(error)); }'
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
CODE_MODE_BRIDGE_ERROR=Error: Unknown tool id: file_write. Did you mean: write? Use openclaw.tools.search to find a tool, openclaw.tools.describe to inspect it, then openclaw.tools.call with the exact id or name.
RAW_TOOL_CALL_ERROR=Unknown tool id: file_write. Did you mean: write? Use tool_search to find a tool, tool_describe to inspect it, then tool_call with the exact id or name.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The same guessed `file_write` id now returns caller-appropriate recovery guidance in both production runtime surfaces. Code Mode no longer instructs the child process to use raw structured tool names that are not available inside the Code Mode bridge.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a full local gateway chat with a live model performing the memory-flush self-correction loop end-to-end. The local proof executes the production Tool Search and Code Mode bridge runtime path that generates the model-visible recovery error, which is the failing behavior called out in review.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Fixed for the current head by adding after-fix local runtime proof for the production Tool Search / Code Mode bridge path. The proof boundary remains disclosed above because no full live model/gateway memory-flush loop was run.
- **RF-002:** Fixed with redacted terminal proof captured from the runtime command above; it prints only the two model-visible recovery errors and no secrets.
- **RF-003:** Fixed with after-fix Tool Search and Code Mode bridge runtime proof rather than only focused Vitest / `git diff --check` output.
- **RF-004:** Fixed by making the shared formatter caller-specific: Code Mode bridge recovery text now uses `openclaw.tools.search`, `openclaw.tools.describe`, and `openclaw.tools.call`.
- **RF-005:** Fixed by executing the production Tool Search catalog and Code Mode bridge path locally and capturing the exact recovery text emitted after the patch.
- **RF-006:** Fixed by pairing the contributor-supplied runtime proof with the small caller-specific recovery-text change.
- **RF-007:** Fixed at the source formatter/lookup boundary; raw Tool Search callers keep raw control-tool guidance, and Code Mode bridge callers keep Code Mode-specific recovery guidance.
- **RF-008:** Fixed by running the requested command after the patch:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93374

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  99 passed (99)
   Start at  03:59:37
   Duration  20.30s (transform 5.37s, setup 864ms, import 9.31s, tests 18.20s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 31.30s
```

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/tool-search.test.ts` for the Code Mode bridge guessed-id regression and existing raw Tool Search guessed-id behavior; `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` for the timeout normalization contract exercised by the required suite.
- **Scenario locked in:** Code Mode calls `openclaw.tools.call("file_write", ...)`, the catalog contains `write`, and the error must suggest `write` plus Code Mode bridge recovery APIs. Existing raw Tool Search coverage continues to lock the raw `tool_call` guessed-id path.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression reaches the production catalog/bridge behavior that constructs the model-visible error while avoiding a broad end-to-end live-model loop; it proves the exact recovery contract that broke without introducing aliases or silently executing guessed tools.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts
# passed: 2 files, 99 tests

git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
# passed: no output
```

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability, automation, security-boundary, auth-provider, public contract, compatibility/default behavior.
- **Risk explanation:** The merge risk is limited to error text for unknown tool ids with suggestions and timeout classification for raw QuickJS runtime interrupts. Public contract surface was reviewed because `openclaw.tools.*` is a caller-visible API string, but this patch preserves the existing canonical API and changes only which already-existing recovery path is named for each caller. Related open PR / same-contract scan boundary: the existing-PR diagnostic found this PR as the linked repair for #92273 and no broader canonical replacement was identified in the local repair context.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is size S, has a focused regression plus production runtime proof, and keeps compatibility/default behavior stable: raw Tool Search callers still receive raw control-tool instructions, Code Mode bridge callers still use the documented bridge API, no config/schema/protocol migration is introduced, and out-of-scope tool execution behavior is intentionally left unchanged.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause was that the shared unknown-tool formatter had only one recovery instruction string even though OpenClaw exposes Tool Search through two different caller contracts. Structured Tool Search controls must mention raw control tools, but Code Mode bridge callers cannot call those raw tool names from inside the child process; their source-of-truth API is `openclaw.tools.*`. A required-suite failure also exposed a Code Mode timeout contract boundary: raw QuickJS runtime `interrupted` errors can escape through the worker top-level catch for async hostile loops, causing the host to see `internal_error` / `interrupted` instead of the runtime timeout contract.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix moves recovery guidance selection to the lookup/formatter boundary that owns the caller contract, so the model-visible error is correct before it is returned to the raw Tool Search or Code Mode caller. It also normalizes raw QuickJS runtime interrupts at the worker/host boundary where the timeout contract is defined, while preserving guest-thrown `Error("interrupted")` as an internal guest error. This fixes the source invariant instead of masking the symptom after the model has already consumed the wrong recovery text.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High; the current HEAD has fresh runtime proof, the requested focused Vitest shard, `git diff --check`, and a regression that locks the reviewed Code Mode bridge failure mode.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected from caller-contract text, timeout normalization, and merge-risk keywords. They are not symptom masking: the formatter now receives the canonical caller surface before constructing the returned error, and the timeout change preserves the existing Code Mode timeout contract for raw VM interrupts rather than changing retry behavior or hiding execution failures.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth owner remains the caller surface contract. Raw structured Tool Search uses raw control tool names; Code Mode bridge uses `openclaw.tools.*`. This patch does not change public config, schema, protocol, migration, default behavior, or compatibility aliases. The public contract scope is minimal/canonical: only the returned recovery text points to the already-existing API for the active caller, and out of scope are new tool names, compatibility shims, or broader tool lookup semantics.

target.title: feat(feishu): extract option, options, form_value from card action callbacks
target.body:
## Problem

When users interact with Feishu interactive card components like `select_static` (dropdowns), `checkbox` (multi-select), or `form` (form submissions), the selected values are lost because `handleFeishuCardAction` only extracts data from `action.value`.

Feishu sends selection data in separate fields:
- `action.option` — for `select_static` single selection
- `action.options` — for `checkbox` / `multi_select` multiple selections  
- `action.form_value` — for `form` submission data

These fields are completely ignored by the current implementation, making it impossible for AI Agents to know what the user actually selected.

Related issue: #42754

## Solution

1. **Extended `FeishuCardActionEvent` type** to include `option`, `options`, and `form_value` fields
2. **Updated content extraction logic** to merge these fields with `action.value` when present, using priority: `form_value` > `options` > `option` > existing behavior
3. **Maintained full backward compatibility** — existing button interactions (`value.text`, `value.command`) work exactly as before

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/card-action.ts` — Type definition + extraction logic (14 lines changed)
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.card-action.test.ts` — 4 new test cases (90 lines added)

## Test Plan

- [x] `select_static` with `option` field — value merged into JSON output
- [x] `checkbox` with `options` array — values merged into JSON output  
- [x] `form` with `form_value` object — values merged into JSON output
- [x] Existing button with `value.command` — backward compatible, unchanged behavior
- [x] All 6 tests pass (`vitest`)
- [x] TypeScript compilation passes

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with assistance from an AI coding assistant (Claude/Cursor). All code changes have been reviewed and tested.

target.title: fix(auth-profiles): import legacy auth-profiles.json into SQLite store on load
target.body:
## Problem

After upgrading an install that stored credentials in per-agent `auth-profiles.json` to a 6.x build, **every agent fails with `401 invalid api key`** for any provider whose credentials resolve through an auth profile.

The runtime auth-profile store now loads exclusively from the per-agent SQLite store (`auth_profile_store` in `<agentDir>/openclaw-agent.sqlite`). The legacy `auth-profiles.json` → SQLite import only runs through `openclaw doctor --fix`, behind an interactive prompt. So after a normal upgrade the SQLite store is empty:

- `openclaw models auth list --agent <id>` → `Profiles: (none)`
- `resolveAuthProfileOrder` returns `[]` (empty `store.profiles` → empty `storeProfiles` → empty `baseOrder`)
- the provider can't resolve its referenced profile → `401 invalid api key` on every agent

The credentials in `auth-profiles.json` are still valid — they are just never read at boot.

## Root cause

`loadAuthProfileStoreForAgent` (`src/agents/auth-profiles/store.ts`) already back-fills the legacy `oauth.json` into an empty SQLite store via `mergeOAuthFileIntoStore`, but there is **no equivalent back-fill for `auth-profiles.json`** (the flat api_key/token profiles). They only get imported by the `doctor --fix` migration.

## Fix

Mirror the existing oauth back-fill: add `mergeLegacyJsonProfilesIntoStore(store, agentDir)` (`persisted.ts`) and call it alongside `mergeOAuthFileIntoStore` when the SQLite store is empty, persisting the result one-shot.

The comprehensive `doctor --fix` migration (backup + delete the JSON + AWS-SDK markers + OAuth sidecar handling) is unchanged; this only adds a lazy safety-net for the common api_key/token case and does **not** delete the JSON.

## Reproduction

Minimal (added as a test): an agent dir with an `auth-profiles.json` api_key profile + empty SQLite store → `loadAuthProfileStoreForRuntime(agentDir).profiles` is empty before the fix, and contains the profile (persisted to SQLite) after.

Real: upgraded a production install (9 agents) from `2026.5.28` to `2026.6.6`. Every agent returned `All models failed: ... 401 invalid api key`; `auth_profile_store` had 0 rows; `paste-api-key` / `doctor --fix` populated SQLite and restored auth (survives restart).

## Testing

- New test in `profiles.test.ts` (`legacy auth-profiles.json lazy migration`): passes with the fix, fails without it.
- `tsc` typecheck and `oxlint` clean on the changed files.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(control-ui): add cron calendar timeline view
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a 24h timeline strip to the Cron Jobs page in the Control UI, providing an at-a-glance view of today's scheduled tasks.

Closes #40644

## Screenshots

### Dark Theme — Full View
![Full page dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dark-full.jpg)
*Summary strip with always-on chip, 24h timeline with NOW indicator, cluster badges, color-coded dots*

### Cluster Hover — Overlapping Jobs
![Cluster hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/cluster-hover.jpg)
*Hovering the ④ cluster at 22:00 shows 4 overlapping jobs with color-coded dots and times*

### Single Dot Hover — Job Info Popup
![Dot hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dot-hover.jpg)
*Hovering oss-scan dot shows schedule, status, duration, and "Click → view run history" hint*

### Light Theme
![Light theme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/light-theme.jpg)
*Full light theme support via existing CSS variables*

## Features

### Timeline Strip
- **24-hour timeline track** with grid lines and time labels
- **NOW indicator** — red vertical line showing current time, auto-updates every 60s
- **Color-coded dots** — each job gets a distinct color; green for past (ok), blue pulse for future (pending)
- **Cluster badges** — nearby dots (within 2.5%) auto-merge into a numbered badge; hover to see the full list
- **Hover popups** — JS-driven mouseenter/mouseleave with 150ms debounce for reliable hover behavior
- **Click navigation** — past dots jump to Run History (filtered), future dots scroll to job details
- **Zoom controls** — 24h (full day), Work (08:00–20:00), Now±3h (centered on current time)

### Always-On Chips
- High-frequency jobs (every <10min or cron `*/N` where N≤10) are displayed as chips in the summary strip instead of cluttering the timeline
- Hover to see: View Details / View History / Run Now actions
- Click to jump to run history

### Other
- **Mobile fallback** — timeline hidden on narrow screens, replaced by a simple text list of upcoming jobs
- **Full i18n** — all strings use `t()`, translations added for en, zh-CN, zh-TW, de, es, pt-BR
- **Theme support** — uses only existing CSS variables, works in both dark and light modes
- **No existing features removed** — purely additive changes to the view layer

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/helpers/cron-timeline.ts` | **New** — Timeline computation (zoom, clustering, high-freq detection) |
| `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` | Added timeline rendering between summary and workspace |
| `ui/src/styles/components.css` | Added timeline CSS (523 lines) |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts` | Added `cron.timeline.*` keys to all 6 locales |

## Testing

- Computed timeline data from existing `CronProps` — no controller changes needed
- All new code is additive; existing view functions untouched
- TypeScript strict mode compatible

---

*AI Disclosure: This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude). All code was reviewed and tested by a human.*

target.title: fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `event.messages` was documented as a generic user-reply channel, but only `/new` and `/reset` actually drained those messages.
- Why it matters: `message:received` hooks could push reply text that was silently dropped, while other lifecycle hooks looked supported even though they had no safe reply target.
- What changed: added a shared internal hook reply-drain helper, kept reset/new command behavior, and now deliver `message:received` hook replies when a routable reply target exists.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not make bootstrap, preprocessed/transcribed, sent, or other lifecycle-only hooks auto-reply.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39688
- Related #40185

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Internal hook replies pushed to `event.messages` are now delivered on `message:received` when OpenClaw has a reply-capable routing target.
- Docs now explicitly limit `event.messages` auto-delivery to reply-capable surfaces instead of implying it works for all hooks.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): internal hooks / inbound reply routing
- Relevant config (redacted): default hook + routeReply test harnesses

### Steps

1. Register an internal hook that pushes text into `event.messages`.
2. Trigger it from `/new` or `message:received`.
3. Verify the reply is routed only when a safe reply target exists.

### Expected

- Reply-capable surfaces route the accumulated hook messages.
- Lifecycle-only surfaces remain non-replying unless they gain an explicit delivery contract later.

### Actual

- Before this fix, `message:received` hook replies were silently dropped.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `commands-core` still routes reset/new hook replies; `dispatch-from-config` now routes `message:received` hook replies on routable surfaces and skips non-routable ones.
- Edge cases checked: non-routable surfaces do not auto-send hook replies; docs/comments now match that contract.
- What you did **not** verify: live messaging-provider delivery outside the existing routeReply test harnesses.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert `fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces`
- Files/config to restore: `src/auto-reply/reply/internal-hook-replies.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-core.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`, `docs/automation/hooks.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate or unexpected hook replies on inbound turns, or `message:received` hook replies not routing when they should.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `message:received` hook replies use the same route-reply path as other provider-routed responses, so a bad target selection would misroute the hook message.
  - Mitigation: the helper uses existing inbound routing fields (`OriginatingTo`, `To`, channel normalization) and adds explicit tests for routable and non-routable surfaces.
- Risk: this branch also carries a whitespace-only `CONTRIBUTING.md` formatter cleanup required for `pnpm check`.
  - Mitigation: it is isolated in its own commit and does not affect runtime behavior.

target.title: fix: add clear separator between metadata and user message
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a user message is passed to the LLM, the metadata blocks (sender info, conversation info, etc.) and the actual user message content are joined with only double newlines. This causes the LLM to sometimes misinterpret the message structure, treating the metadata as the entire message and ignoring the actual user content ("message is empty" replies).
- **Why it matters:** Users intermittently get incorrect "message is empty" responses even though their message was correctly stored in session history.
- **What changed:**
  - `runtime-context-prompt.ts`: When inbound metadata is present, `buildCurrentInboundPrompt()` injects `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator between the context prefix and the user prompt.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.ts`: Add `stripUserMessageSeparator()` post-processing in both `stripInboundMetadata` (webchat/API/gateway) and `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` (TUI) to remove the separator after metadata blocks are stripped. Gated on `didStripMetadata` flag for defense-in-depth.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.test.ts`: 7 new tests covering separator stripping and legacy preservation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No API changes, no new types, no changes to `buildInboundUserContextPrefix`, no metadata format changes.

### Why `---openclaw:user-msg---` instead of `---\n**User Message:**`

The separator is a program-generated marker that **cannot exist** in any legacy stored message or user-typed content. This eliminates the need for complex detection logic (`_sep` markers, `seenUserContent` flags, structural JSON parsing) and makes legacy message compatibility trivial: the stripper only removes `---openclaw:user-msg---`, which legacy data never contains.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- LLM now receives a clear `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator between metadata and user content, reducing "message is empty" false positives.
- The separator is stripped in all user-facing surfaces (TUI, webchat, gateway API, session cost logs).
- Legacy messages (pre-upgrade) are completely unaffected — the unique marker cannot appear in historical data.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04)
- Runtime: Node v24.15.0, pnpm 10.23.0

### Steps

1. Send a message to the LLM through any channel with metadata (group chat, sender info, etc.)
2. Observe the prompt construction in `buildCurrentInboundPrompt()`
3. Verify the separator appears between metadata and user content
4. Verify `stripInboundMetadata` / `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` removes the separator in user-facing views

### Expected

- LLM correctly distinguishes metadata from user message content
- No separator visible in TUI/webchat/gateway/session logs

### Actual

- Confirmed working as expected in unit tests and live CLI verification (see Real behavior proof below)

## Evidence

- [x] Passing tests: 39 strip-inbound-meta tests pass (7 new separator-specific tests)
- New tests cover: separator after single/multiple metadata blocks, no-metadata preservation, non-separator `---` preservation, sentinel-without-metadata-block edge case, legacy message preservation

## Real behavior proof

Live CLI verification on the rebased branch (`b69cb795`):

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Metadata and user message content are joined with only double newlines, causing the LLM to misinterpret the message structure and ignore actual user content.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw development setup on Ubuntu 24.04 with Node v24.15.0.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. `npx tsx proof-test.mjs` - Live CLI verification of separator injection and stripping
2. `pnpm test strip-inbound-meta` - Unit tests for the new separator logic

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== Real Behavior Proof: User Message Separator ===

1. buildCurrentInboundPrompt WITH metadata:
---
Conversation info (untrusted metadata):
```json
{"channel": "#general", "sender": "Alice"}
```

---openclaw:user-msg---
Hello, how are you?
---
✅ Separator ---openclaw:user-msg--- is present between metadata and user message

2. buildCurrentInboundPrompt WITHOUT metadata:
---
Hello, how are you?
---
✅ No separator injected when metadata is absent

3. stripInboundMetadata (metadata + separator):
Input: (metadata + separator + user message)
Output: Hello, how are you?
✅ Metadata and separator both removed

4. stripLeadingInboundMetadata (metadata + separator):
Input: (metadata + separator + user message)
Output: Hello, how are you?
✅ Metadata and separator both removed in leading-only strip mode

5. Legacy message (no metadata, user has --- in content):
Input: Hello\n\n---\nSome user markdown
Output: Hello\n\n---\nSome user markdown (preserved intact)
✅ Legacy user content preserved, no false stripping

=== All checks passed ===
```

All tests (39 total) pass.

**Observed result after fix:** The separator is correctly injected when metadata is present and correctly stripped in all user-facing surfaces. Legacy messages without metadata are completely unaffected.

**What was not tested:** Live end-to-end with real LLM provider (simulated in unit tests).

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: metadata with separator injection, separator stripping after single/multiple metadata blocks, preservation when no metadata present, preservation of `---` in non-separator context
- Edge cases checked: legacy messages with `---\n**User Message:**` user content (preserved intact), sentinel text without metadata fences, user content starting with generic `---`
- What you did **not** verify: Live end-to-end with real LLM provider

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — legacy messages are unaffected; the unique marker cannot appear in pre-upgrade data
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit
- Files/config to restore: `runtime-context-prompt.ts`, `strip-inbound-meta.ts`
- Known bad symptoms: `---openclaw:user-msg---` appearing in TUI/webchat/session history

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: LLM treats `---openclaw:user-msg---` as confusing noise
  - Mitigation: The marker reads like a boundary tag; LLMs routinely handle similar delimiters in system/user prompts. It's a single line and less disruptive than the metadata blocks themselves.

target.title: fix(cron): allow timeoutSeconds: 0 for no-timeout mode
target.body:
## 🐛 Problem

The cron job UI validation logic incorrectly rejects `timeoutSeconds: 0`, which should represent "no timeout" mode.

**Current behavior:**
```typescript
if (timeout <= 0) {
  errors.timeoutSeconds = "cron.errors.timeoutInvalid";
}
```
This prevents users from setting `timeoutSeconds = 0` to disable timeout entirely.

## ✅ Solution

Update validation to allow `0` as a valid value (meaning "no timeout"):

```typescript
// 0 = no timeout, > 0 = timeout in seconds
if (timeout !== 0 && timeout <= 0) {
  errors.timeoutSeconds = "cron.errors.timeoutInvalid";
}
```

## 📁 Files Changed

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.ts` - Updated `validateCronForm` function

## 🧪 Testing

- Create a cron job with `timeoutSeconds: 0`
- Verify it saves without validation errors
- Confirm the job runs without timeout restrictions

## 📊 Impact

- **User-facing**: Users can now disable timeout for long-running cron jobs
- **Backward compatible**: Existing jobs with `timeoutSeconds > 0` are unaffected
- **Risk**: Low - single line change, clear semantic intent

## 📝 Related

Closes #41272

target.title: fix(discord): preserve content-type and skip optimization for animated/WebP images
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Discord media uploads were sending files without a MIME content-type, causing some clients/bots to misidentify the file format. Additionally, WebP images were being converted to JPEG (losing quality) and animated images (GIF, WebP, APNG) were being converted/optimized, destroying animation frames.
- **Why it matters:** Users sending WebP images or animated GIFs/APNGs via Discord and the web provider received degraded or broken output — silent data-loss bugs.
- **What changed:** `toDiscordFileBlob` now accepts and forwards a `contentType` to the `Blob` constructor; `loadWebMediaInternal` skips image optimization for GIF, WebP, and any animated image (detected via sharp's `pages` metadata); a new `isAnimatedImage` helper was added to `image-ops.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** Non-animated JPEG/PNG optimization paths are unchanged; no other channels are affected.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #41256
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- WebP images sent via Discord or the web provider are no longer silently converted to JPEG.
- Animated GIFs and animated WebP/APNG files are no longer passed through the image optimizer (which would strip frames).
- Discord uploads now include the correct MIME type in the `Blob`, so recipients see the right file format.
- Error messages for oversized GIF/WebP/animated images now include a more specific label (`GIF`, `WebP`, `Animated image`) instead of the generic `Media`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / Bun
- Model/provider: Any
- Integration/channel: Discord
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Send an animated GIF or a WebP image to a Discord channel via OpenClaw.
2. Observe the uploaded file in Discord.

### Expected

- Animated GIFs play in Discord.
- WebP images display as WebP (not JPEG).
- File MIME type is correctly reported.

### Actual (before fix)

- WebP images were converted to JPEG.
- Animated WebP images lost their animation frames.
- `Blob` had no `type`, so Discord received files with an unknown MIME type.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — new tests added in `src/discord/send.shared.test.ts`, `src/media/image-ops.helpers.test.ts`, and `src/web/media.test.ts` covering all three fix areas.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: _fill in_
- Edge cases checked: static WebP (should still skip optimization), single-frame GIF
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord upload end-to-end (depends on bot credentials)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert `src/web/media.ts` to re-enable optimization for WebP/animated; revert `src/discord/send.shared.ts` to drop content-type from `Blob`.
- Files/config to restore: `src/web/media.ts`, `src/discord/send.shared.ts`, `src/discord/send.components.ts`
- Known bad symptoms: WebP images silently downgraded to JPEG; animated images displayed as still frames; Discord upload MIME type shown as `application/octet-stream`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Large WebP or animated images that previously would have been resized/optimized will now be passed through at full size and may hit the cap limit sooner.
  - Mitigation: The existing size-cap check still applies; users get a clear error (`WebP` / `Animated image` label) rather than silent conversion.

target.title: feat(cli): support --file for image generate command
target.body:
## Summary
The `image generate` CLI command was missing the `--file` option that `image edit` already supported. This PR adds `--file` support to achieve parity, forwarding input files to the image generation runtime.

Fixes #91734.

## Changes
- `src/cli/capability-cli.ts`:
  - Added `"--file"` to `image.generate` capability metadata flags
  - Added `.option("--file <path>", "Input file", collectOption, [])` to the `image generate` command registration
  - Wired `opts.file` through to `runImageGenerate({ file: files })`

- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`:
  - Added regression test: `forwards input files for image generate`
  - Added regression test: `reports the expanded image.generate flags in capability inspect`

- `docs/cli/infer.md`:
  - Common tasks table now notes `--file` for `image generate`
  - Added `image generate --file` example in the code block
  - Notes section clarifies `image generate --file` vs `image edit` usage

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts --run` — 97 passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json src/cli/capability-cli.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` — clean
- `pnpm build` — clean
- `pnpm format:fix` — clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw infer image generate --file <path>` now accepts and forwards input files to the generation runtime, matching the existing `image edit --file` behavior.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node.js 22, local source checkout, real test image file.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Verify `image generate --help` lists the new `--file` option:
     ```bash
     OPENCLAW_PROFILE=dev node scripts/run-node.mjs infer image generate --help
     ```
  2. Run `image generate` with an actual file path and prompt:
     ```bash
     OPENCLAW_PROFILE=dev node scripts/run-node.mjs infer image generate --file /home/0668000666/0668000666/AI/OpenClaw/test.jpeg --prompt "a blue sky" --json
     ```
  3. Inspect the published capability flags:
     ```bash
     OPENCLAW_PROFILE=dev node scripts/run-node.mjs capability inspect --name image.generate --json
     ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  Screenshot showing `image generate --help` with the `--file` option:
  ![image generate --help showing --file option](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11ea03f0-611e-48be-9fb5-33fbb9f59a04)

  Screenshot showing `image generate --file` executed against a real local file:
  ![image generate --file execution](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77b23b09-a281-4e66-b890-f4af18b39a5a)

  Screenshot showing `capability inspect --name image.generate` reporting the new `--file` flag:
  ![capability inspect showing --file flag](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96f0acaf-5d79-4522-93d9-6f7b48cef55e)
- **Observed result after fix**:
  - `image generate --file <path>` is accepted by the CLI parser.
  - The file path is forwarded to the image generation runtime; the command reaches the provider dispatch layer and returns a normal "No image-generation model configured" runtime error, **not** an `unknown option` parse error.
  - `capability inspect --name image.generate` reports `--file` in the capability flags.
- **What was not tested**: Live provider image generation with actual API keys and model configuration; only CLI parsing, capability metadata, and runtime forwarding are verified.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: config: use datetime suffix for config backup files instead of numeric rotation
target.body:
Closes #39923

## Summary

Replaces the numeric rotation ring (`openclaw.json.bak.1`–`.bak.4`) with datetime-stamped backup files (`openclaw.json.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHmmssSSS`, UTC). This makes it immediately clear *when* each backup was taken without needing to inspect file metadata — directly useful when recovering from a bad config change.

**Before:**
```
openclaw.json.bak
openclaw.json.bak.1   ← newest rotation slot
openclaw.json.bak.2
openclaw.json.bak.3
openclaw.json.bak.4   ← oldest rotation slot
```

**After:**
```
openclaw.json.bak
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-143022456   ← most recent
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-130015000
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-110000000
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-090000000   ← oldest kept
```

## Changes

- `formatBackupTimestamp(date)` — new helper producing `YYYYMMDD-HHmmssSSS` (UTC, millisecond precision to avoid collision on rapid saves; lexicographically sortable without reading file metadata)
- `BACKUP_DATETIME_RE` — exported regex for matching valid datetime suffixes
- `rotateConfigBackups` — now renames `.bak` → `.bak.<timestamp>` instead of chaining renames across numbered slots; accepts optional `now` param for deterministic testing
- `hardenBackupPermissions` — scans directory for datetime-pattern `.bak.*` entries and chmods them (in addition to the primary `.bak`)
- `cleanOrphanBackups` — replaces the `validSuffixes` integer set with datetime-pattern matching; deletes all non-datetime `.bak.*` files (including legacy `.bak.1`–`.bak.4`); keeps only the `CONFIG_BACKUP_COUNT - 1` most recent datetime backups via lexicographic sort
- `maintainConfigBackups` — passes `now` through to `rotateConfigBackups`; `readdir` promoted to required on `BackupMaintenanceFs` (production `fs.promises` always has it)

## Test plan

- `formatBackupTimestamp` — UTC formatting, padding, lexicographic sort order, regex match
- Ring test — 6 writes with fixed timestamps confirms 4 datetime backups kept, oldest pruned, `.bak` holds most-recent-minus-one
- `hardenBackupPermissions` — verifies 0o600 on both `.bak` and `.bak.<datetime>` (POSIX only)
- `cleanOrphanBackups` — legacy numeric/PID/manual orphans deleted; datetime files preserved; limit enforcement with `CONFIG_BACKUP_COUNT + 1` overflow
- `maintainConfigBackups` — full compose flow: prior `.bak` gets a datetime stamp, new `.bak` created, permissions hardened, orphans pruned

target.title: feat(feishu): comprehensive plugin enhancements — streaming, dedup, skills, calendar, and stability
target.body:
## Summary

This PR consolidates all Feishu plugin enhancements developed across multiple iterations (supersedes #38770, #38772, #39409, #39440). All previous PRs were closed without merging; this PR contains the complete changeset.

---

## Issues Fixed

### Streaming Card Display

1. **Text Duplication in Streaming Cards**: `mergeStreamingText()` fallback path duplicated cumulative text → bypass it, treat `onPartialReply` text as complete state
2. **Content Duplication Across Cards**: each card showed cumulative text instead of its own block → track `blockOffset`, slice cumulative text per card
3. **Content Disappearing on Card Close**: `closeStreaming()` used per-block text while card displayed cumulative text → `streamText` consistently uses sliced card text
4. **Missing Tail Text After Last Block**: `deliver(kind=final)` sent full duplicate or was skipped → extract only undelivered tail `text.slice(blockOffset)`, send if non-empty
5. **Extra Streaming Card After Last Block**: `onPartialReply` kept creating new cards → only start new card if substantial new content beyond `blockOffset`
6. **Slow Streaming Updates**: `await this.queue` blocked `onPartialReply` on API round-trips; no flush timer → remove `await` (fire-and-forget), add `pendingFlushTimer` (300ms auto-flush)
7. **Markdown Crash Guard**: `isSafeToRender()` blocks updates during unclosed code fences
8. **Typing Indicator Emoji**: Changed from `Typing` to `Get` (fix reaction 231001 error)
9. **Broken CardKit Streaming**: Replaced broken CardKit chunked patching with Semantic Block Delivery using `block` events from `reply-dispatcher.ts`

### Message Deduplication (3-Layer)

10. **event_id Dedup**: Lark SDK `EventDispatcher.parse()` flattens header into callback data — `event_id` IS available. Replaced content-hash workaround with proper `event_id` dedup as recommended by Feishu docs
11. **Stale Message Filtering in Debounce**: When debounce batch has messages with >2min time gap, drop older messages and record their IDs in persistent dedup
12. **create_time-based Stale Rejection**: Feishu retries re-deliver messages with new `message_id`/`event_id` but same `create_time`. Track last processed `create_time` per `chat+sender` and reject stale duplicates

### Message Control & Abort

13. **Fast Abort Bypass**: Trigger words (`stop`, `停止`, `取消`, `esc`, etc.) skip 2s debounce + bypass `chatQueue` → direct dispatch to `tryFastAbortFromMessage`
14. **Message Recall Abort**: Register `im.message.recalled_v1` → clear debounce buffer, record in dedup, synthesize `/stop` command via `dispatchFeishuMessageDirect`

### Feishu Ops Tools (Skills)

15. **feishu_contacts**: Search contacts with auto-sync from API
16. **feishu_groups**: List/search bot groups with auto-sync
17. **feishu_send**: Send text/file/@all to users or groups
18. **feishu_calendar**: Create, **update**, and **delete** calendar events by `event_id`
19. **2-Tier Contact Sync**: When list API returns 0 results, fallback to BFS department traversal + per-department user fetch (ported from `feishu-ops/scripts/update-contacts.py`)
20. **SKILL.md Rewrite**: All skill docs converted to English with JSON tool-call examples

### Code Quality

21. **Error Messages Standardized to English** across all new modules
22. **Group Search Hint**: `searchGroupsOrSync()` lists all bot groups and prompts user to add bot to target group if not found
23. **Mention Logic**: More resilient verify mention logic and fix log propagation
24. **SDK v1.30+ Response Parsing**: Fix `proactive-send.ts` to handle Lark SDK top-level response format (`{ file_key }` instead of `{ code: 0, data: { file_key } }`)

### Other

25. **Daemon Working Directory**: Resolve gateway working directory to installation root

---

## Files Changed

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` | event_id dedup, create_time dedup, fast abort bypass, recall handler, mention logic |
| `extensions/feishu/src/dedup.ts` | `checkAndRecordCreateTime()`, create_time tracking per chat+sender |
| `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts` | Block-boundary card splitting, blockOffset tracking, tail text, semantic block delivery |
| `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts` | Remove mergeStreamingText, async queue, pendingFlushTimer, isSafeToRender |
| `extensions/feishu/src/typing.ts` | Change typing emoji to Get |
| `extensions/feishu/src/inbound-debounce.ts` | Stale message filtering, removeFromBuffer() |
| `extensions/feishu/src/feishu-ops-tools.ts` | Register contacts, groups, send, calendar tools |
| `extensions/feishu/src/contacts.ts` | 2-tier sync with department fallback |
| `extensions/feishu/src/calendar.ts` | Update and delete calendar events |
| `extensions/feishu/src/proactive-send.ts` | SDK v1.30+ response parsing fix |
| `extensions/feishu/skills/*/SKILL.md` | English rewrite with JSON examples |
| `src/daemon/program-args.ts` | Working directory resolution fix |

## Supersedes

- #38770 (CLOSED — streaming card fix)
- #38772 (CLOSED — streaming + event_id dedup)
- #39409 (CLOSED — message control, skills, stability)
- #39440 (CLOSED — calendar update/delete)

target.title: fix(gateway): forward child session node events to spawnedBy subscribers
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: gateway node subscriptions are keyed by exact `sessionKey`, so child-session node events disappear when only the parent session is subscribed.
- Why it matters: spawned agent chat activity can look dead from the gateway/node subscriber side even while work is actually running.
- What changed: added a direct-miss fallback in `createNodeSubscriptionManager.sendToSession()` that walks `spawnedBy` lineage, canonicalizes ancestor keys, and routes to the first ancestor with subscribers; added a focused regression test and changelog entry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change ACP execution, session transcript bridging, or broader subagent progress/status surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #34852
- Related #34905

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Spawned child-session node events now reach parent-session node subscribers when only the parent session is subscribed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway node subscriptions
- Relevant config (redacted): default test harness

### Steps

1. Subscribe a node to a parent session key.
2. Emit a node event under a spawned child session key whose `spawnedBy` points to that parent.
3. Observe delivery through the node subscription manager.

### Expected

- Parent subscriber receives the child-session event.

### Actual

- Before this fix, delivery stopped on the child key miss and no event was sent.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: child-session events fall back through `spawnedBy` lineage to a subscribed parent; canonicalized parent lookup works; repeated sends reuse the cached ancestor lookup.
- Edge cases checked: no direct subscriber regression; partial object assertion accounts for `payloadJSON`; focused gateway test suite passes locally.
- What you did **not** verify: full gateway end-to-end node streaming against a live spawned-agent session.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR to restore exact-key-only subscription routing.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-subscriptions.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: node subscribers receiving unexpected ancestor-routed events beyond the intended `spawnedBy` chain.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: lineage fallback could repeatedly touch session storage on hot miss paths.
  - Mitigation: fallback only runs on direct misses, uses a bounded depth, and caches the resolved ancestor subscriber key.

Supersedes the dirty branch state in #34905.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize mangled tool names and IDs from OpenAI-compati…
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Some OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. Kimi) send tool call IDs like `functions.exec:0` as `functions exec:0` (space instead of dot) and tool names like `exec` as `functions exec`. Tool result matching fails, causing "Tool functions exec not found" errors.
- **Why it matters:** Users on Kimi and similar providers cannot complete tool-using flows; matching is done by ID and name, and the mangled forms did not match.
- **What changed:** Added `normalizeMangledToolCallId()` in `tool-call-id.ts` to rewrite `functions ` -> `functions.` in IDs; use it in `extractToolResultId()` and as the key in `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist()` so mangled and canonical IDs map to the same sanitized value. Added `normalizePrefixedToolName()` in `session-transcript-repair.ts` to strip `functions.` / `functions ` from tool names; applied in assistant block handling, `hasToolCallName`, and `normalizeToolResultName()` so tool-result names are normalized and pairing works.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No change to provider clients or wire format; only transcript repair and ID sanitization for matching.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39091
- Related: normalizeToolResultName fix included

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Tool-using flows with OpenAI-compatible providers that mangle tool IDs/names (e.g. Kimi) now match tool results to tool calls correctly; "Tool functions exec not found" is resolved.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any
- Provider: OpenAI-compatible (e.g. Kimi) that sends `functions exec:0` / `functions exec` style IDs and names

### Steps

1. Run agent with such a provider; trigger a tool call (e.g. exec).
2. Before fix: tool result may not match, "Tool functions exec not found" or similar.
3. After fix: IDs and names normalize; pairing and sanitization map mangled and canonical forms to the same value.

### Expected

Tool results pair with assistant tool calls; no spurious "tool not found" from ID/name mangling.

### Actual

As expected after the fix.

## Evidence

- `normalizeMangledToolCallId` and `extractToolResultId` / `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist` tests in `tool-call-id.test.ts`.
- `sanitizeToolUseResultPairing` test with mangled ID and prefixed name in `session-transcript-repair.test.ts`.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Unit tests added; logic follows issue and Greptile feedback (compare original rawToolName in normalizeToolResultName, use normalized ID as map key).
- **Edge cases checked:** Empty string, already-canonical IDs, names without prefix.
- **What you did not verify:** Live Kimi or other provider E2E.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Revert the commit.
- Files: `src/agents/tool-call-id.ts`, `src/agents/session-transcript-repair.ts`.

## Risks and Mitigations

**None.** Normalization is additive and only affects matching/sanitization; no new execution paths.

target.title: feat(agents): per-agent outbound A2A allowlist override
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `agents.list[].tools.agentToAgent.allow` to control which agents a specific agent may contact via session tools (`sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `session_status`)
- When unset, the agent falls back to the global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` list; when set to explicit IDs, only those targets are reachable; `[]` isolates the agent from all cross-agent access
- The global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` continues to act as a participation gate (both requester and target must match it) before per-agent outbound rules are evaluated
- Outbound-only semantics: only the requester's override is checked, not the target's
- Replace `matchesAllow` with `matchesGlobalParticipation` and add `evaluateAccess()` returning a structured `AgentToAgentDenyReason` so all call sites produce context-aware error messages via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`
- Scrub deleted agent references from per-agent outbound allowlists in `pruneAgentConfig()`

### Example config (hub-spoke pattern)

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] }
  },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "david",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["*"] } } },
      { "id": "spoke",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } },
      { "id": "kelly",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } }
    ]
  }
}
```

spoke→david ✅ | spoke→kelly ❌ | david→anyone ✅

## Test plan

- [x] `sessions-access.test.ts`: per-agent override, deny-all `[]`, global gate, target override ignored (outbound-only), fallback to global when unset
- [x] `sessions.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny during label lookup in `sessions_send`
- [x] `openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny for cross-agent `session_status`
- [x] `agents.test.ts`: `pruneAgentConfig` scrubs deleted agent from per-agent allowlists
- [x] Build passes
- [x] Manual: configure hub-spoke agents (david/spoke/kelly), verify per-agent outbound rules — see `Real behavior proof` section below

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: per-agent outbound `agentToAgent.allow` correctly gates cross-agent session tool access. Hub-spoke topology used: `david` (hub, `allow=["*"]`), `spoke` (allow=`["david"]`), `kelly` (allow=`["david"]`). Validates the new `evaluateAccess()` decision pipeline reaches the documented per-agent outbound semantics, with the structured `per_agent_outbound` deny reason surfaced via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`.

- **Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 22.04 (kernel 5.15), Node 22.22.0, pnpm 10. Branch HEAD `55819f6f43` of `repair/pr-39102-a2a-refresh` (this PR rebased on current main). Live invocation of the production export `createAgentToAgentPolicy` from `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — no mocks, no test runner, the same code path that backs `sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, and `session_status` at runtime.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  1. Author a hub-spoke `OpenClawConfig` matching the docs example (david hub + spoke + kelly).
  2. Build `policy = createAgentToAgentPolicy(cfg)`.
  3. Call `policy.evaluateAccess(requester, target)` for the five edge pairs.
  4. Run with: `pnpm tsx proof-hub-spoke.ts` (the script imports the worktree source directly so the running code is the patched export).

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output, copied verbatim from `node` stdout via `tsx`.

  ```
  # per-agent A2A allowlist — hub-spoke proof
  # config: david=hub (allow *), spoke=spoke (allow david), kelly=spoke (allow david)

  [ok ] spoke -> david: ALLOWED
  [ok ] spoke -> kelly: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)
  [ok ] david -> kelly: ALLOWED
  [ok ] david -> spoke: ALLOWED
  [ok ] kelly -> spoke: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)

  all cases match expected behavior
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: all five real invocations matched the documented hub-spoke semantics. Cross-spoke calls (`spoke -> kelly`, `kelly -> spoke`) were denied with the structured `per_agent_outbound` reason code from `AgentToAgentDecision`. Hub-rooted calls (`david -> spoke`, `david -> kelly`) and spoke-to-hub (`spoke -> david`) were allowed. Process exit code: `0`. Console output above is the literal stdout, no post-processing.

- **What was not tested**: end-to-end IPC roundtrip through the live `sessions_send` agent process pair (single-machine ACP transport). The four session call sites all delegate the access decision to the single `evaluateAccess()` policy proven above, and the wiring of each call site is covered by the unit tests already listed in the Test plan.

target.title: Security: harden gateway timeouts and auth store sealing
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `GatewayClient.request()` could hang indefinitely, auth stores were always plaintext at rest, and mirrored transcript writes did not re-assert restrictive permissions after append.
- Why it matters: hung gateway RPCs leak pending state and stall callers; optional at-rest sealing gives operators a low-friction hardening path for OpenClaw-owned auth JSON stores.
- What changed: added bounded gateway request timeouts, added optional `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` sealing for `auth-profiles.json` and legacy `oauth.json`, added regression coverage, and re-applied `0600` perms after mirrored transcript writes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not implement full session transcript encryption because `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` and multiple local readers still depend on raw JSONL session files.
- Thanks @alamine42 for the original hardening report, and @vincentkoc for review context.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #4954
- Related #7604

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `GatewayClient` requests now fail with `gateway request timeout for <method>` instead of hanging forever.
- When `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` is set, OpenClaw-owned auth stores are written in sealed form and require that same env var to be read.
- Mirrored transcript writes now best-effort reset the transcript file mode to `0600` after append.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  Optional auth-store sealing adds a new env-based operator hardening path. The format is backward compatible because plaintext reads still work when sealing is not enabled, and encrypted reads fail closed with a warning if the passphrase env var is missing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: n/a
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): optional `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE`

### Steps

1. Run the focused test suite for gateway client, auth store, oauth migration, and transcript paths.
2. Run repo-level `pnpm check`.
3. Run repo-level `pnpm build`.

### Expected

- Gateway requests time out cleanly.
- Auth stores round-trip in sealed form when `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` is set.
- Build and lint stay green.

### Actual

- Verified locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused Vitest coverage for gateway timeouts, sealed auth-store save/load, sealed oauth migration, and transcript append behavior.
- Edge cases checked: timeout cleanup removes pending entries; encrypted auth stores do not contain plaintext secrets; legacy oauth migration still works when the source file is sealed.
- What you did **not** verify: full transcript encryption, because it is explicitly out of scope for this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  Operators who want auth-store sealing can set `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` before writing credentials and keep it set for future reads.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: unset `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` before writing new auth store files, or revert the branch.
- Files/config to restore: `auth-profiles.json` / `oauth.json` from backup if an operator loses the passphrase.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: warning that an encrypted auth store requires `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` when the env var is missing.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator enables auth-store sealing and later starts OpenClaw without `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE`.
  - Mitigation: reads fail closed with a targeted warning instead of silently parsing garbage.
- Risk: timeout defaults could surface latent long-running RPC assumptions.
  - Mitigation: timeout is bounded but configurable per client/request.
